name: Air Elemental
description: These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . An great Air Elemental and many minions were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. This creature is a massed congelation of air and vapors shaped into a head, torso, and huge, powerful arms. Despite their size, its body and arms are able to move with very high speed and accuracy. The master Elemental is able to channel magical currents through its arms to project a very long-ranged cone of freezing air. The minions must rely on closing the distance to their prey and swinging.

name: Air Elemental Minion
description: These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . An great Air Elemental and many minions were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. This creature is a massed congelation of air and vapors shaped into a head, torso, and huge, powerful arms. While the master can employ spellcraft through its arms, the minions must rely on closing the distance to their prey and swinging.

name: Alpha Wolf
description: The alpha wolf is larger and tougher than an average gray wolf and projects a deep, luminous blue aura. An alpha leads a pack of gray wolves that range through the wooded country around Stonebridge . Its pack is located just beyond the farmlands, where the woods closes in around the path to the crypts. Another one ranges on the Peninsula and can be found among some ruins where Elddim Lowlands borders the Great Northern Forest .

name: Ancient Corpse
description: Ancient Corpse Affiliations Wesrin Cross Hovart's Folly Undead Regular Damage h2h 14-16 lvl 5 Armor 57 Experience 40 Veteran Damage h2h 96-156 lvl 59 Armor 590 Experience 26,600 Elite Damage h2h 155-232 lvl 92 Armor 914 Experience 737,160 An Ancient Corpse is an undead enemy encountered in Dungeon Siege I .

name: Ancient Scorpiot
description: This wood and steel automaton resembles a scorpion. It has two spells: Rcannon , which produces a fireshot effect, and Lcannon , a burst-fire chaingun that cannot be dodged (both base 30-45dmg).

name: Angler
description: These brightly-colored water single-eyed creatures have many cousins around the continent. On the Island of the Utraeans , they dwell in flooded subterranean areas and on beaches.

name: Arakun Spider
description: Arakun Spider Race Spider Affiliations Wesrin Cross Hovart's Folly Seck Regular Damage h2h 15-17 lvl 3 Armor 25 Experience 33 Veteran Damage h2h 115-157 lvl 57 Armor 273 Experience 21,945 Elite Damage h2h 169-249 lvl 90 Armor 423 Experience 608,157 The Arakun Spider is an arachnid foe encountered in Dungeon Siege .

name: Arhok Cold Storage Cave
description: Arhok Cold Storage Cave Map Have a world map of this location? Then please upload it! Type Region Arhok's Cold Storage Cave , in which a frost giant had made its lair by the time of the quest to retrieve the Staff of Stars . The cave contains frozen meats on the hangers and corpses of guards, butchers and mutilated mules. Ice Golems , Yeti , and an Ice Giant roamed the cave after slaughtering the survivors of the caravan that was carrying the Staff of Stars. At the end of the cave is a functional Displacer that leads to Arhok.

name: Automaton Blade
description: This is a bulky-looking humanoid automaton with twin blades. There are several variants of this foe, and their attack sequence is very slow.

name: Automaton Mech
description: This is a humanoid automaton equipped with dsx_spell_automoton_minigun (88-95dmg), which is functionally a burst-fire gatling gun.

name: Bandit
description: Bandit Affiliations Humans Forests Regular Damage as weapon + (37 - 42) Armor 175 Experience 1300 Veteran Damage as weapon + (170 - 250) Armor 595 Experience 67,600 Elite Damage as weapon + (258 - 378) Armor 864 Experience 1,795,300 Bandit s are a type of enemy in Dungeon Siege . They are among the very few evil-aligned Humans in the game. Bandits are encountered in the Dark Forest of Ehb and in an analogous setting on the Utraean Peninsula , the Cloud Forest . This article describes the basic melee fighter bandit. The Bandit classes are:

name: Bandit Archer
description: Bandit Archer Affiliations Humans Forests Regular Damage (as weapon) + 50-60 lvl 14 Armor 150 Experience 1,138 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 293-456 lvl 64 Armor 526 Experience 59,176 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 462-707 lvl 97 Armor 765 Experience 1,571,578 Bandit Archer s are a type of enemy in Dungeon Siege . They are among the very few evil-aligned Humans in the game. Bandits are encountered in the Dark Forest of Ehb and in an analogous setting on the Utraean Peninsula , the Cloud Forest . This article describes the ranger-class bandit. The Bandit classes are:

name: Bandit Boss
description: This bandit has twice the health of the Rogue Bandit , another bandit miniboss. He is also set apart from most other boss-level creatures as having a guaranteed rare/unique drop. The bandit boss is the presumed overlord of bandits in a region, and is a uniquely-tough individual able to preserve order among evil men. The boss has a unique face mask to mark his status at the top of the chain. His appearance is similar to that of a death-metal rocker, being decked in studded, skull-adorned, red-and-black clothing, sporting tattoos and a mohawk hairstyle, and his instrument of choice is an axe.

name: Barkrunner
description: Barkrunner Affiliations Fen of the Frozen Regular Damage h2h 45-57 lvl 8 Armor 102 Experience 183 Veteran Damage h2h 182-282 lvl 50 Armor 471 Experience 39,894 Elite Damage h2h 276-428 lvl 78 Armor 715 Experience 1,065,060 Barkrunners are a group of towering humanoid tree-like creatures. They have an incredibly dense skin that are made up of dense barks, allowing them to endure being hacked by swords, pierced by arrows, or being attacked with spells. The barkrunners have glowing red eyes and having large limbs that allow them to bash their foes with incredible strength. Their tree-like bodies are perfect for blending in with the midst of trees and bushes. Barkrunners can be found in the forested borderlands of the Fen of the Frozen . Nature mages can summon them at a certain level, and at higher levels even transform into them.

name: Battle Wraith
description: Battle Wraiths are melee fighters of a somewhat greater caliber than "ordinary" Wraiths. Their fist-fighting style borders on berserk, and they are even known to remove their helmets, with their skull still in it, flailing wildly at a target. Like other undead beings, they are single-minded and fearless. Wraiths (in general) are cursed spirits that have been animated by a nearby evil presence or the necromantic arts. The lower half of their body is gone and the creature is essentially a skull, arms, ribcage, and spine. It floats in the air and moves with much greater speed and purpose than a full-bodied Skeleton .

name: Bear
description: Bear Have a picture of Bear? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies While not being a mini boss, the Bear is a unique enemy since he can only be found in a cave early in the game.

name: Bile Lunger
description: Without a glowing miniboss aura, this miniboss closely resembles a Water Slinger . He is based on the Lunger type, though, so he uses hand-to-hand combat. The creature drops either a magic armor piece or a wildcard armor, weapon, ring, or amulet when defeated.

name: Black Drake
description: Black Drake Affiliations Drakes Vault of Eternity Volcanic Caverns Regular Damage claw & breath + (229 - 280) Armor 554 Experience 170,000 Veteran Damage claw & breath + (341 - 510) Armor 1,175 Experience 510,000 Elite Damage claw & breath + (465 - 694) Armor 1,601 Experience 15,810,000 The Black Drake is a hostile class of Drake in Dungeon Siege . Black Drakes have mottled black scales. They attack foes either by breathing white-hot flames or closing in with powerful claws. The magic of Black Drakes can be studied by very high level combat mages and be cast at Level 104 in the skill. The Molten and Black are the top drakes; the former is tougher and the latter hits harder. Their experience value is enormous. Given enough mana, Level 94 combat mages can summon one to fight on their behalf.

name: Black Skrubb
description: Black Skrubb Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 0 Armor 52 Experience 20 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 172-293 lvl 0 Armor 557 Experience 17,080 Elite Damage (as spell) + 336-428 lvl 0 Armor 908 Experience 549,640 Black Skrubb s are a low level enemy encountered in the earlier stages of Dungeon Siege .

name: Black Wolf
description: Black Wolves inhabit the deep-forest areas of Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula . These are the top dogs. They are a larger and more dangerous strain than their cousins in other climes. The mottled-black coat on this creature suggests that they were nocturnal hunters. During the action in Ehb and the Peninsula, however, woodsmen were alarmed by a shift in the wolves' behavior, where they started roaming about in large packs even during daylight and became very bold towards humans. The Dark Wolf, the strongest individual wolf anywhere, is a miniboss black wolf.

name: Blaster
description: This is a treaded robotic tank mounting a grenade launcher. The Blaster is very heavily armored and clearly built by the Goblins for warfare. It may have domestic capacities, such as mining and excavation, when operating inside their warrens.

name: Blazing Elemental
description: This small, non-sentient fire elemental is seen in the battle with Gom , who has these and other manifestations of fire at his beck. The Blazing Elemental is barely over knee-high. It races up to the legs of the nearest enemy and detonates with great force and heat.

name: Blue Drake
description: There are a few instances of this type of forest-dwelling drake in Ehb and the Peninsula , where they are treated as capital encounters. Its blue breath weapon has a base damage of 145-155.

name: Blue Shard
description: This is a Shard -type creature of medium size and strength encountered in the crystal cave environments. It is stronger than the Teal , sturdier but not as damaging as the Green , and inferior to the Purple . Its spell, shard_zap_blue , is a blue homing-projectile attack similar to that of the other shards.

name: Bog Beast
description: Bog Beast Affiliations Regular Damage h2h 48-57 lvl 9 Armor 105 Experience 184 Veteran Damage h2h 194-282 lvl 51 Armor 513 Experience 40,112 Elite Damage h2h 295-428 lvl 79 Armor 779 Experience 1,070,880 Bog Beasts are an enemy found on the Island of the Utraeans . They appear to be an analogue to the Swamp Creatures of Aranna . It's unknown if the bog beasts are related to the Larches as well.

name: Bonepicker's Trading Post
description: Bonepicker's Trading Post Have an image of this location? Then please upload it! Map Have a world map of this location? Then please upload it! Bonepicker's Trading Post is a trio of three houses that lay beside a lake in the coniferous forest near the Temple Ruins in Ehb . One of the houses is operated as a general merchandise shop by the bonepicker Thayne and his assistant Gregor . Another one of the houses contained a doorway that led down into a waterlogged chamber where three interesting things were found: lots of deadly creatures, three dead treasure hunters and their mule , and a Goblin contraption that operated a secret door to a treasure chest.

name: Bones
description: This is the earliest skeleton revenant in the expansion campaign. It has 8 DEX as opposed to the original Skeleton 's 3, has more life and is worth more xp. It bears neither armor nor weapon. It uses its decidedly bony fists to make flailing attacks at the player.

name: Bookas
description: Bookas Affiliations Island of the Utraeans Guliks Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 6 Armor 62 Experience 88 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 95-208 lvl 48 Armor 336 Experience 30,712 Elite Damage (as spell) + 158-347 lvl 76 Armor 517 Experience 949,520 The Bookas is a pygmy spellcaster in the Dark Jungle near Illicor on the Island of the Utraeans .

name: Braak
description: The Braak are a race of cloven-footed, horned humanoids that dwell in the cold mountains of northern Ehb , as well as the neighboring Utraean Peninsula . There is a powerful desert-dwelling strain, as well. Braak generally have blue skin, blood-red eyes, and sharp teeth. Their forearms and lower legs are covered in white fur, and they usually wear a furry pelt over their torso to keep warm. They are social and semi-intelligent, as evidenced by their cooperative behavior and clothing. The eponymous Braak is encountered in cold environments, and is a melee combatant often backed up by Braak Mages .

name: Braak Mage
description: The Braak Mage is a smaller variant of Braak that uses magic to assault its foes, rather than melee attacks. They can be found throughout the forests of Frostspire Mountain in northern Ehb , and the snowy mountains of the Utraean Peninsula .

name: Brankar the Scavenger
description: Brankar is larger than ordinary krug and has an odd blue-greenish hue to his skin. Like other krug scavengers, though, he wears only a ragged loincloth, is poorly-armed, and does little damage. Though able to lead groups of krug scavengers in their efforts to despoil the countryside, Brankar is not as strong in combat as a plain Krug Grunt . He may also lack what little discipline would be required to operate within a krug army.

name: Bronze Gargoyle
description: This is a ranged magic user found in the swamplands of Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula . They are leagues ahead of their crypt-dwelling jade-throwing counterparts , with more health, and their eyes shoot a long-ranged laser beam ( spell_gargoyle_zap_bronze , base 74-76dmg) that is relatively powerful and always hits.

name: Cave Angler
description: This creature dwells in dim, flooded depressions in some of the Island of the Utraeans dungeons. It is encountered in large groups.

name: Cave Bear
description: It's a bear.

name: Cave Giant
description: No Cave Giants are encountered in any of the official campaigns. The normal-mode stats given here serve as the base creature, commensurately boosted by skill ranks, for the Cave Giant summoned by the eponymous Nature spell .

name: Chitterskrag
description: These monsters resembled the cross between the hermit crab and the cockroach that can grow several meters tall and similarly wide. According to old documents in Jherkal's Crown , they were a threat to cities even in the golden days of the empire. Their chitin carapace can be fashioned into medium armor, suited to rangers. They usually come in a large group and can easily overwhelm the adventurers if they're not careful.

name: Chomper
description: Apparently named after its front incisor, this is a squat, melee-bound sentient creature found in the beaches and caves of Island of the Utraeans . A later and smaller cousin can be found in the Ztrool .

name: Cicatrix
description: This greater boss monster is targeted in The Scourge of Cicatrix (Quest) . Cicatrix is called "Lord of the Undead" and owns a lovely swampside dungeon on the Island of the Utraeans . The undead lord has the appearance of a centaur skeleton which he presumably sustains, undying, with his own black arts. His form and equipment emit a flaming aura of power, and he prefers to inhabit the lava-lit depths of his mountain. Cicatrix bears a heavy plate cuirass, Death Knight Shield, and a Tusk Helm. In combat he wields a flaming sickle and throws mass skeleton-summoning spells, producing melee Reapers and archers that bear the title "Cicatrix' Skeleton".

name: Clockwork Automaton
description: This is a larger, decorated model of the Automaton Blade which guards the innermost facility of the Great Clock .

name: Cobble Man
description: The Cobble Man has levels in combat magic which make its ranged attack effective in the expansion, and the original Rock Beast does not. It uses a similar large, conical rock spray spell, spell_rock_beast_blast_dsx (105-124).

name: Copter
description: This is a small point-defense robot created by the Goblins to defend their warrens from intruders. It flies by means of propellers, fins, and sophisticated internal gadgetry. The Copter mounts a Lightning Gun and can recognize foes according to the programming of its goblin creators. The copter is designed to hover above the melee and pressure the target, while bigger and better-armored creatures and equipment get in close. Alone, the object poses little threat.

name: Crystal Golem
description: There do not appear to be any instances of this creature in the campaign settings. The texturing for it appears unfinished, and its ability scores are nonsensical. The elite hand-to-hand attack, 7000 damage applied with a skill of 160, is about fifteen times more powerful than the peninsula's greatest pugilist, Colonel Norick . It has a duplicate creature template, the Diamond Golem ; this one being labeled _one , and the other _two .

name: Crystal Guardian
description: Crystal Guardian Affiliations Shadowjumper Fortress Emarard Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 19 Armor 350 Experience 1,826 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 162-223 lvl 62 Armor 1,010 Experience 71,214 Elite Damage (as spell) + 264-364 lvl 90 Armor 1,420 Experience 1,902,692 Crystal Guardian Affiliations Shadowjumper Fortress Emarard Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 19 Armor 375 Experience 1,826 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 177-242 lvl 62 Armor 1,079 Experience 71,214 Elite Damage (as spell) + 290-396 lvl 90 Armor 1,517 Experience 1,902,692 Crystal Guardian Affiliations Shadowjumper Fortress Emarard Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 19 Armor 400 Experience 1,826 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 217-294 lvl 62 Armor 1,148 Experience 71,214 Elite Damage (as spell) + 355-482 lvl 90 Armor 1,614 Experience 1,902,692 Crystal Guardian Affiliations Shadowjumper Fortress Emarard Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 20 Armor 500 Experience 1,826 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 198-300 lvl 64 Armor 1,424 Experience 71,214 Elite Damage (as spell) + 324-491 lvl 92 Armor 2,002 Experience 1,902,692 The Crystal Guardians were magical constructs spawned by the Shadowjumper to prevent practical progress of the pursuing party of the Hero of Arhok . He left them in the lava caves beneath Fortress Emarard to block access to the displacer leading to Mount Kreth and the Great Clock . Each one was tied to an impenetrable mass of crystal that blocked the path; to clear the jam, its respective Guardian had to be destroyed. The Hero was clued in to their existence by the Goblin merchant Neener , and their destruction was the last recorded quest in the campaign journal. They resembled oversized Shards, and attacked the same way. There were four of them, in order of weakest to strongest: one purple, one yellow, one orange, and one ruby.

name: Cyclops
description: Cyclops Affiliations Regular Damage (as weapon) + 85-95 lvl 24 Armor 255 Experience 12,446 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 279-404 lvl 68 Armor 585 Experience 124,460 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 434-614 lvl 100 Armor 817 Experience 3,186,176 The Cyclops is a kind of massive giant with a single eye. They have pale skin due to living in dark forest with limited sunlight. Cyclopes wear nothing but loincloths, yet are rather durable. They can be found lurking in the depth of dark forests like those near Bonepicker's Post or Redwood Gap , armed either with their fists or with an appropriately-titled Massive Club.

name: Dark Acolyte
description: Dark Acolyte Have a picture of Dark Acolyte? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Dark Acolyte is a wraith-like undead that dwells in the Fen of the Frozen. They are floating skeletons without lower bodies. These undead wears their horned helmets. When provoked, they will lash out with their claws.

name: Dark Caster
description: Dark Casters inhabit western Aranna's crystalline caves in colonies with Dark Lungers and Dark Slingers . They employ an instantaneous-hit spell, dark_lunger_zap , which is based on the Nature entry-spell Zap.

name: Dark Generator
description: Dark Generators were mago-technological devices used by the Shadowjumper to channel his power and rapidly move around. They darkened the immediate area around them, possibly allowing the Shadowjumper even more power.

name: Dark Klaw
description: Dark Klaw Affiliations Klaws Regular Damage 46 - 54 Armor 154 Experience 190 Veteran Damage 245 - 351 Armor 1,005 Experience 41,420 Elite Damage 373 - 535 Armor 1,530 Experience 1,105,800 The Dark Klaw is a miniboss version of the Klaw, a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege . In contrast to the drab-colored alpine Klaws, the Dark Klaw has bleached horns and distinctive markings in its hide. It has a 20% chance of dropping a magic item when defeated.

name: Dark Lunger
description: Dark Lungers inhabit western Aranna's crystalline caves in colonies with Dark Slingers and Dark Casters . They attack by closing in on the player and clawing their hands.

name: Dark Mucosa
description: Dark Mucosa Affiliations Hovart's Folly Wesrin Cross Regular Damage h2h 45-49 lvl 0 Armor 75 Experience 61 Veteran Damage h2h 292-409 lvl 0 Armor 672 Experience 28,975 Elite Damage h2h 489-591 lvl 0 Armor 1,040 Experience 789,645 The Dark Mucosa is a large red-colored melee-fighting Mucosa found early on in Dungeon Siege. It is something of an "elite infantry", boasting better stats than its beige cousin as well as most other enemies found where it is.

name: Dark Slinger
description: Dark Slingers inhabit western Aranna's crystalline caves in colonies with Dark Lungers and Dark Casters . They attack with a low-damage, rapid-fire projectile spell, dark_lunger_spear , which resembles a forked javelin.

name: Dark Wolf
description: The Dark Wolf is a guardian spirit of black wolves, the extra-large and ferocious wolf breed inhabiting the deep forests. The creature's hide hangs rather loosely in places; the sinew of its legs and ribs are exposed, its fleshless skull is fully-exposed as well, and its eye sockets glow red. While the Dark Wolf is clearly not of the living, it leads its descendants on hunts through the forests of the Utraean Peninsula .

name: Dark Wraith Mage
description: The Dark Wraith is a powered-up Wraith Mage. It radiates a rust-colored aura of power and casts the homing projectile spell wraith hands . There are two of these creatures; one in Ehb and the other on the Utraean Peninsula , and both of them were evidently victims of the Furies , as they are in the monsters' general proximity. In these ways the Dark Wraith Mage is similar to the Battle Wraith miniboss, the Warrior Wraith .

name: Dark Zepheryl
description: Dark Zepheryl Affiliations Tower of Kmethekt Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 15 Armor 107 Experience 520 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 134-252 lvl 57 Armor 386 Experience 47,320 Elite Damage (as spell) + 224-419 lvl 85 Armor 567 Experience 1,266,200 Zepheryls are a class of entity composed of purple magical energy that attack enemies by zapping them. Level 24 nature mages can summon them for themselves, given enough mana. They have a smaller cousin in the form of the basic Zepheryl . They are perhaps related to Demlock's Orb spells.

name: Darkling
description: Darkling Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 10 Armor 38 Experience 85 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 97-143 lvl 64 Armor 268 Experience 29,665 Elite Damage (as spell) + 128-212 lvl 98 Armor 463 Experience 917,150 A Darkling is a creature encountered in Dungeon Siege .

name: Death Knight
description: Death Knight Affiliations Mountain of the Dead Lair of Cicatrix Cicatrix Regular Damage (as weapon) + 68-78 lvl 11 Armor 128 Experience 287 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 449-648 lvl 53 Armor 554 Experience 46,494 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 713-1017 lvl 81 Armor 836 Experience 1,243,571 Death Knights are ancient Utraean warriors under servitude to Cicatrix . They are armed with Utraean Plate, a Hack, and a Metal Buckler. Combat mages of level 20 and higher can summon ones of their own.

name: Demlock's Cut
description: Demlock's Cut Map Have a world map of this location? Then please upload it! Type Region Demlock's Cut was a geographical feature located in northeastern region of the Island of the Utraeans . It consists of a deep canyon surrounded by a temperate biome similar to that of Elddim or Stonebridge , but heavily steeped in magic. It is accessible by displacer , so the region likely the campus of the Tower of Kmethekt before the gouge was created. The deepest part of the Cut is known as Demlock's Ravine .

name: Demlock's Ravine
description: Demlock's Ravine Map Have a world map of this location? Then please upload it! Demlock's Ravine is a geographical feature located in Demlock's Cut in the Island of the Utraeans . It is the deepest of the many valleys and canyons created around the Tower of Kmethekt , when the Utraean high wizard Demlock cast massive thunderstorms before his death in order to drown the oncoming Zaurask hordes. By the time the Hero of Arhok arrived at the ravine, evil cultists had set up camp, and were summoning Rune creatures . These efforts of theres culminated in their leader conjuring the Rune Guardian: a massive specimen of Rune monsters, equipped with the Scythe of Angk , the tempestuous first magical weapon created by Utraean smiths. The trader Lhuk Torath was separated from his father Bran by these abominations, and the Hero and his companions had to push through many spellcasters and summoned beasts before they were able to see Lhuk. In their way, however, stood the Rune Master and his Guardian, plus many more casters and acclimated Rune creatures. They did kill every Rune-associated monster, loot the Master's treasure , and acquire the Scythe of Angk for their own use. Lhuk was then able to trade with the adventurers. He had set up near a Displacer pad , which linked with the Tower of Kmethekt's exit to Xot's Pass . Demlock's Ravine, like the rest of the Cut, shares a similar climate to the temperate regions of Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula , only with much more rain and a color-changing sky; both caused by powerful magic.

name: Desert Braak
description: Desert Braak Affiliations Braak Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe Canyons Regular Damage 122 - 228 Armor 365 Experience 39,484 Veteran Damage 205 - 469 Armor 725 Experience 157,936 Elite Damage 284 - 649 Armor 1,004 Experience 3,672,012 The Desert Braak is a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege . These wooly, ramlike humanoids walk upright and appear to be social. Desert Braak throw boulders via the spell braak boulder . Not to be mistaken for conventional spellcasters, they are extremely durable.

name: Doppelganger
description: Doppelganger Affiliations Doppelgangers Tower of Kmethekt Regular Damage h2h 80-86 lvl 13 Armor 174 Experience 748 Veteran Damage h2h 231-303 lvl 55 Armor 598 Experience 68,068 Elite Damage h2h 337-443 lvl 83 Armor 873 Experience 1,821,380 Doppelgangers are enemies encountered in Legends of Aranna inside the Tower of Kmethekt . Doppelgangers themselves have no attack. When they sight an enemy, they immediately transform into a copy of him/her and engage with the weapons that the target has.

name: Doppelganger Knave
description: Doppelganger Knave Affiliations Doppelgangers Tower of Kmethekt Regular Damage h2h 66-72 lvl 14 Armor 156 Experience 692 Veteran Damage h2h 184-245 lvl 56 Armor 522 Experience 62,972 Elite Damage h2h 267-356 lvl 84 Armor 758 Experience 1,685,020 Doppelganger Knaves are simply a tougher version of the normal Doppelganger .

name: Doppelganger Lord
description: Doppelganger Lord Affiliations Tower of Kmethekt Doppelgangers Regular Damage h2h 83-89 lvl 13 Armor 184 Experience 920 Veteran Damage h2h 239-313 lvl 57 Armor 628 Experience 63,480 Elite Damage h2h 343-450 lvl 85 Armor 903 Experience 1,685,440 The Doppelganger Lord is the strongest of the Doppelgangers that can be found in the Tower of Kmethekt . It guards the exit to Xot's Pass and the displacer back to Lhuk Torath , along with four Knaves . Aside from its higher stats, the Lord can be distinguished by having a green aura in addition to the normal blue.

name: Drake
description: The Drake is an enemy creature class in Dungeon Siege . Various breeds of drakes are encountered, sporadically, from the snowy areas around Fallraen / Glacern onward; to end-stage areas like the Castle Ehb dungeons and Volcanic Caverns . Undead drakes, known as Rahks , can be found on the Island of the Utraeans in the Lair of Cicatrix .

name: Droc
description: Droc Droc concept art, as seen in the Legends of Aranna manual. The Droc are a primitive crocodilian race, uplifted by Nosirrom , presumably from crocodiles. He sought to use them as extra numbers by which he could dominate Island of the Utraeans .

name: Droc Spatter
description: Droc Spatter Droc Spatter is a reddish Droc that capable of spitting out yellow orb out of its maw. Their ranged attack allows the Droc Spatter to keep its distance away from the player.

name: Droc Superior
description: Droc Superior Affiliations Xot's Badlands Regular Damage h2h 105-117 lvl 18 Armor 184 Experience 717 Veteran Damage h2h 288-392 lvl 61 Armor 609 Experience 65,247 Elite Damage h2h 409-557 lvl 88 Armor 866 Experience 1,745,895 Droc Superior is a stronger version of Droc. This creature carry a large skull and use it as a mace. The Droc Superior have a marking around the head and have slightly darker green scales.

name: Droog Archer
description: Droog Archer Affiliations Droog Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe & territory Regular Damage 20 - 36 Armor 300 Experience 34,600 Veteran Damage 111 - 238 Armor 740 Experience 173,000 Elite Damage 193 - 386 Armor 1031 Experience 5,051,600 A Droog Archer is a hostile, ranged-type Droog in Dungeon Siege .

name: Droog Captain
description: The Droog Captain is a toughened-up version of a Droog Grunt . Supposedly this creature leads the most xenophobic faction of Droog warriors, rallying them out in the bush to disrupt foreign incursions and traffic through Droog territory. His most detested enemy is probably the 10th Legion and anyone purporting to work on its behalf. As opposed to having a typical fuzz of bluish hair, the Captain appears to have shaved his head and is covered from the head down in unique tattoo patterns; this could perhaps be a symbol of seniority.

name: Droog Grunt
description: Droog Grunt Affiliations Droog Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe & territory Regular Damage as weapon + (40 - 60) Armor 460 Experience 46,179 Veteran Damage as weapon + (138 - 278) Armor 998 Experience 184,716 Elite Damage as weapon + (228 - 438) Armor 1390 Experience 4,294,647 A Droog Grunt is a hostile, melee-class Droog in Dungeon Siege .

name: Droog Mage
description: Droog Mage Affiliations Droog Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe & territory Regular Damage as spell + (102 - 158) Armor 275 Experience 34,600 Veteran Damage as spell + (171 - 324) Armor 596 Experience 173,000 Elite Damage as spell + (237 - 449) Armor 825 Experience 5,051,600 A Droog Mage is a hostile Nature mage Droog in Dungeon Siege .

name: Droog Village (Cliffs of Fire)
description: Droog Village (Cliffs of Fire) Map Have a world map of this location? Then please upload it! Map Kingdom of Ehb Transitions Cliffs of Fire Type Town Environment Arid regions Population Nonataya Crusader Goquua Droog villagers Enemies Droog fanatics Estimated Enemies 10 The Droog Village is a town of Droog that lies within the Cliffs of Fire . A party of adventurers led by a former farmer cleaved their way through the town's army, and eventually pushed their way into the village. Here, after receiving a surrendering welcome, they fought past an ambush staged by the most vicious of the Droog warriors before reaching their chieftain Nonataya . Once they learned the truth about why the Droog were fighting them, and about the circumstances of their failure, the party worked to save the village and its surviving population from the great dragon Scorch , who the Seck had awakened in his cavernous lair . Structurally, the village is of identical configuration to the town of Quillrabe in the Utraean Peninsula . It differs in other regards, however; for example it has a much less diverse population (consisting solely of Droog), and it is located at the fortified top of a canyon rather than at the bottom, which exchanges facilitation of commerce and closer control over its river for immediate security against attack.

name: Dungeon Crawler
description: Dungeon Crawler Affiliations Undead Regular Damage 156 - 200 Armor 260 Experience 50,000 Veteran Damage 245 - 384 Armor 482 Experience 200,000 Elite Damage 336 - 526 Armor 660 Experience 4,650,000 The Dungeon Crawler is a hostile Undead creature in Dungeon Siege . The Dungeon Crawler appears to be a type of zombified human corpse in an advanced state of decay, with the lower half of its body missing. This does not stop the upper half from dragging itself along the ground and attempting to grab, thrash, and bite anything within reach. This creature moves too slowly to pose any threat unless there are additional pressures and distractions in the area preventing people from standing out of its way; in which case, beware its powerful melee attacks.

name: Dust Spider
description: Dust Spider Affiliations Halls of the Lost Regular Damage h2h 17-20 lvl 3 Armor 19 Experience 25 Veteran Damage h2h 143-206 lvl 45 Armor 203 Experience 20,375 Elite Damage h2h 229-330 lvl 73 Armor 325 Experience 687,050 Dust Spider Have a picture of Dust Spider? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Dust Spider is a species of spider in Dungeon Siege, a cousin of the Arakun Spiders found in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula .

name: Earth Elemental
description: These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . An immense Earth Elemental and two minions were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. This creature is a massive bipedal amalgamation of rock and ore, a sort of elder brother to Rock Beasts . The elementals are of a far taller and heavier stature and have random ore deposits jutting out of their main bulk. The rock comprising the being seems able to magically re-morph itself as it moves and attacks. As one might expect of solid rock, it is sluggish but can deal a stupendous amount of damage when it lands a hit.

name: Earth Elemental Minion
description: These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . An immense Earth Elemental and two Minions were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. The Earth Elemental Minion is a miniature, light-duty version of the Earth Elemental— which is to say, it is an enormous, berserk stone animus that can swing its fists with extreme force.

name: Emerald Spider
description: Emerald Spider Have a picture of Emerald Spider? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Emerald Spiders are a group of oversized green spiders that can be found in the caves near Arhok , beyond the Frozen Lake , that lead to the Halls of the Lost . The Emerald Spider is first seen attacking Lyssa by the entrance of the cave.

name: Enraged Gorack
description: This is a miniboss variant of the Gorack . It wears more colorful clothing than its minions and has granite-colored skin. Goracks are themselves among the most dangerous temperate-woodland opponents, and the Enraged Gorack hits even harder and lasts even longer. The Enraged Gorack encounters in the single- and multiplayer worlds are in the Dark Forest and Cloud Forest , respectively, at points that are unlikely to be missed when passing through.

name: Fallraen Forest
description: Fallraen Forest Have an image of this location? Then please upload it! Map Have a world map of this location? Then please upload it! Fallraen Forest is a snowy, coniferous forest in the Utraean Peninsula , near Mount Utrae, that surrounds the town of Fallraen . The snow often whites out vision. Snowflakes fall past snow covered trees, and if you stray from the beaten path it is all to easy to lose ones way amongst the snow-banks, caves, and ancient ruins frozen in time. The navigable portion of the forest is dividable into two separate segments; one below Fallraen that is closer to Crystwind , and one above Fallraen that is closer to Meren . The lower section is the more definitive of the two, containing more actual forest as well as most points of interest, while the upper section i shorter, has a Legion displacer tower, and more main-story quest relevance with connections to caves holding the Fallraen Stone and to the Meren road.

name: Farm Skrubb
description: Farm Skrubb Considered one of the weakest enemies in the game, the Farm Skrubb harrasses the player by shooting dark greenish goo at them. The Farm Skrubb burrows under the ground until prey comes nearby at which point it surfaces and begins attacking.

name: Ferocious Braak
description: This opponent has unusual markings and a face which, in compliance with its name, is simply more ferocious than ordinary braak. The Ferocious Braak in Ehb is inside the caves beyond the Cliff city and Dragon's Rathe , on the way to Castle Ehb. The one on the Utraean Peninsula is guarding a bottleneck near the end of the Quillrabe Canyons , but outside the Drake Nest .

name: Fire Elemental
description: These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . Two huge Fire Elementals were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. This creature is a very tall, almost gangly biped composed of living fire. The fire elemental is in extreme dudgeon for its imprisonment and misuse and will attack.

name: Fire Elemental Minion
description: Fire Elemental Minion Have a picture of Fire Elemental Minion? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Fire Elemental Minion is an enemy encountered at the end of Legends of Aranna . These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . An immense Fire Elemental and two Minions were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. The Fire Elemental Minion is a miniature version of the Fire Elemental .

name: Flechette
description: Flechette Affiliations Goblins Constructs Great Clock Facility Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 20 Armor 207 Experience 2,732 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 124-162 lvl 63 Armor 565 Experience 79,228 Elite Damage (as spell) + 205-268 lvl 91 Armor 788 Experience 2,150,084 The Flechette is a weaker version of the Perforator that can be found in the Goblin fortress surrounding the Great Clock .

name: Fleshrender
description: Fleshrender Have a picture of Fleshrender? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Undead Cicatrix Regular Damage h2h 50-68 lvl 10 Armor 100 Experience 258 Veteran Damage h2h 202-336 lvl 52 Armor 463 Experience 41,796 Elite Damage h2h 307-511 lvl 80 Armor 703 Experience 1,117,914 Fleshrenders are humanoid creatures that can be found lurking in the steamy swamp of Fen of the Frozen . These fiends have grey bodies, blue eyes and a crown of 'spikes' on top their heads. Their heads have strong lower jaw with lower fang sticking out. They seemed to be well adapted to live near the water since they have webbed hands and feet. Level 51 combat mages can summon one to fight on their behalf.

name: Flying Gritch
description: Flying Gritch Affiliations Tower of Kmethekt Regular Damage h2h 65-81 lvl 13 Armor 130 Experience 400 Veteran Damage h2h 187-286 lvl 55 Armor 457 Experience 48,400 Elite Damage h2h 274-417 lvl 83 Armor 667 Experience 1,297,200 Flying Gritches are a species of hostile flying arthropod found in the Tower of Kmethekt . They resemble grayish Soul Stingers.

name: Forest Darkling
description: Forest Darkling Affiliations Redwood Gap Great Northern Forest Temple Ruins Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 23 Armor 390 Experience 12,134 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 119-170 lvl 66 Armor 853 Experience 121,340 Elite Damage (as spell) + 210-300 lvl 99 Armor 1,197 Experience 3,106,304 Forest Darklings are a beefed-up version of Darklings . They can be found in deep forests around Aranna , and they cast a green spark.

name: Forest Klaw
description: This is a predatory, forest-dwelling beast with a large array of natural weaponry, seemingly tuned to the purpose of ripping players up into edible sections. It has three angular horns protruding from its head and large, prominent jaws; its arms are long and wiry and possess the huge claws that probably give the creature its name. The Klaw does not move as fast as the player and can be dodged-against in combat, should its attacks prove to be too much pressure.

name: Forest Phrak
description: Forest Phrak Affiliations Phraks Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 95-105 lvl 12 Armor 100 Experience 2,000 Veteran Damage h2h 190-256 lvl 56 Armor 296 Experience 78,000 Elite Damage h2h 268-362 lvl 89 Armor 419 Experience 2,084,000 Forest Phraks are a Phrak subspecies found near the Temple Ruins in Ehb and in Redwood Gap in the Utraean Peninsula . They yield a large amount of experience for their small health pool, and as such are great for leveling up.

name: Forest Troll
description: The Forest Troll is a large, gangly biped with black fur. They have yellow eyes, small pointy ears, having unusually long forearms, and wearing dark loins. They are capable of talking verbally and being somewhat primitively intelligent, since the adventurers heard them talking about a large telescope in the Dark Jungle . The trolls are very sociable and come in small packs, which can be troublesome for the travelers. Despite being capable of talking, these creatures usually let out the growling and grunting while attacking the adventurers. Encountered in old-growth forests, it appears to be pack-oriented and devoted to attacking travelers in the cramped routes through these areas. To this end it wields a spiked Forest Club which is periodically dropped when the troll is slain. Packs of trolls will prove to be tiresome battles due to their high health ratings and an unusual tactic they employ. During battle a distressed troll is likely to disengage and flee to regenerate health. Players will be disengaged as well when this happens, temporarily, but should beware of giving chase deeper into the woods and dragging in more trolls.

name: Frost Archer
description: The Frost Archer is distinguishable by its deeper hue of blue and sometimes a blue aura. It boasts higher health and damage than its regular counterparts.

name: Frost Drake
description: Frost Drake Affiliations Drakes Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 20 h2h 100-120 lvl 20 Armor 102 Experience 370 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 223-288 lvl 73 h2h 408-598 lvl 73 Armor 534 Experience 44,770 Elite Damage (as spell) + 361-467 lvl 106 h2h 606-888 lvl 106 Armor 794 Experience 1,199,910 Frost Drakes are the earliest and weakest class of drake that can be encountered.

name: Frost Golem
description: Golems are said to be magical constructs. Frost Golems are generally encountered in cold interior zones, and are not as dangerous as Ice Warriors . They attack with an upward swing.

name: Frost Mage
description: By his appearance, the Frost Mage seems to have acquired an abnormal level of comfort with the ice caves. As with the lesser mages, the chief danger posed by this creature comes from his summoned Ice Warriors , which he can summon indefinitely. His own health ratings and the damage of his spell ice blast remain poor. The miniboss is likely to drop a combat magic spell and either a robe or wildcard item of blue quality when slain.

name: Frost Spitter
description: This spider is colored to blend in with the subterranean ice cavern environment. Its uses a projectile frost ray attack, frostnid_freeze (40-44dmg). It's physically identical to a Frostnid .

name: Frost Warrior
description: The Frost Warrior has an opaque appearance and a glowing blue aura of power. Its damage rating is not necessarily greater than that of a regular Ice Warrior, but its life and armor ratings are. The creature is most likely (80%) to drop either blue fighter armor or a blue weapon when destroyed.

name: Frostnid
description: The Frostnid is an Arachnid faced in the colder regions of the world of Ehb . It is a slower moving spider with a simple biting attack.

name: Frostnid Spitter
description: Frostnid Spitter Have a picture of Frostnid Spitter? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Frostnid Spitter Have a picture of Frostnid Spitter? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Frostnid Spitters are a group of oversized spiders that can be found around the Frozen Lake near Arhok . They are essentially a weaker cousin of the Frost Spitters found in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula .

name: Furok
description: The furok is a shaggy, lumbering, snow-bound humanoid based on creatures in folklore, covered in thick white fur and with huge hands to rend its prey apart. It is found in the colder regions of the world and due to its colour it can camouflage itself very well amongst the frozen trees and bushes.

name: Furok Slasher
description: The Furok slasher is a variant of Furok found in the bitterly cold regions of the land of Ehb and the Utraen Peninsula. It differs from the standard Furok with dark black stripes along its arms, legs and upper body, and it is significantly more powerful.

name: Fury
description: Fury Affiliations Bosses Regular Damage as spell + (170 - 200) Armor 450 Experience 31,165 Veteran Damage as spell + (310 - 445) Armor 993 Experience 155,825 Elite Damage as spell + (429 - 617) Armor 1,376 Experience 4,550,090 Fury Have a picture of Fury? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Fury is a boss monster in Dungeon Siege .

name: Fury Spawn
description: Fury Spawn Have a picture of Fury Spawn? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Furies Temple Ruins Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 35 Armor 260 Experience 17,000 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 101-146 lvl 77 Armor 650 Experience 170,000 Elite Damage (as spell) + 179-258 lvl 109 Armor 901 Experience 4,352,000 Fury Spawn are miniature or juvenile versions of the Furies , and as such are weaker. Fury Spawn are able to create a multicoloured light show of piercing energy beams that emerge from the beast’s limbs, similar to their adult form. This attack damages a wide area in front of it, and the beams can either be orange, purple or green. Fury Spawn is described as a small, single-eyed creature with large mouth and several 'arms' that emit powerful energy beams. Fury Spawn can be found lurking in western Aranna and beyond. They can be found at Ehb , inside a cave underneath the Temple Ruins , though they are not present at a similar cave in the Utraean Peninsula .

name: Futak the Trainer
description: Futak the Trainer can be seen as one of the Krug leaders in the assault on Stonebridge , the other one being Huskar the Shaman . Futak the Trainer is equiped with a Hack and Krohars Buckler. It is likely that Krohars Buckler was picked up from a farmer that he killed in a raid, or stolen from the town of Stonebridge during the previous night's fight. Futak the Trainer is essentially a boss Krug. While by himself he does not pose a significant threat, his strongest asset is the numerous Krug surrounding him that can overwhelm the player with numbers. Area effect spells are useful in this battle.

name: Gargoyle
description: Gargoyle Have a picture of Gargoyle? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies

name: Ghastly Necron
description: Ghastly Necron Affiliations Undead Cicatrix Lair of Cicatrix Regular Damage (as weapon) + 70-76 lvl 12 Armor 135 Experience 313 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 434-592 lvl 54 Armor 576 Experience 50,706 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 686-927 lvl 82 Armor 864 Experience 1,356,229 Ghastly Necrons are a kind of undead enemy found in the Lair of Cicatrix . They resemble a purple-and-green-clothed zombified Seck Mage , complete with the staff. Despite this, they do not cast any spells, instead using their staves in melee.

name: Ghost
description: Ghost Affiliations Castle Ehb Castle Hiroth Regular Damage as spell + (170 - 190) Armor 220 Experience 62,000 Veteran Damage as spell + (267 - 365) Armor 436 Experience 186,000 Elite Damage as spell + (366 - 500) Armor 598 Experience 5,766,000 The Ghost is a hostile Undead creature in Dungeon Siege . This non-corporeal evil spirit guards sites of past violence. It lashes out at trespassers with a ranged life-draining attack that cannot be dodged. The Ghost has a substantially lower defense rating than other inhabitants of places where it is encountered. Finally, it has a high likelihood of dropping magic rings or amulets when slain.

name: Giant Chitterskrag
description: Giant Chitterskrag Have a picture of Giant Chitterskrag? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Giant Chitterskrag are an over-sized Chitterskrag and is one of the largest creatures in the Island of the Utraeans , resembling a crab the a size of an elephant. They have large pincers and multiple legs. Their bodies are either grey or blue. They can be found crawling across the jungle-overgrown ruins of Jherkal's Crown , as well as roaming the halls of the Central Transportation Center . The Giant Chitterskrag is the largest of the Chitterskrag family, possibly a fully matured individual.

name: Giant Lizard
description: Giant Lizard Affiliations Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe Canyons Regular Damage 150 - 180 Armor 400 Experience 34,634 Veteran Damage 261 - 383 Armor 777 Experience 173,170 Elite Damage 362 - 531 Armor 1,079 Experience 5,056,564 The Giant Lizard is a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege . This is a horned, quadrupedal reptile that approaches from low on the ground. Slow but resilient, the Giant Lizard was probably docile until malevolent forces crept across Aranna's deserts, after which point it became very grumpy.

name: Giant Moth
description: Giant Moth A giant moth. Giant Moths are an insect enemy faced in the early stages of Dungeon Siege .

name: Giant Rat
description: Giant Rat Affiliations Mines Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 30-32 lvl 6 Armor 35 Experience 42 Veteran Damage h2h 169-226 lvl 60 Armor 266 Experience 27,930 Elite Damage h2h 248-345 lvl 93 Armor 405 Experience 774,018 Giant Rat Affiliations Dungeons Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 23-24 lvl 4 Armor 35 Experience 47 Veteran Damage h2h 165-211 lvl 46 Armor 301 Experience 31,255 Elite Damage h2h 262-334 lvl 74 Armor 477 Experience 866,163 The Giant Rat is an animal enemy faced in the earlier stages of the game.

name: Giant Skeleton
description: Giant Skeleton Have a picture of Giant Skeleton? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Giant Skeleton is, namely, a giant reanimated skeleton , standing around 10 feet tall, possibly even overshadowing the Half-Giants . Giant Skeletons carry a Str 24 Long Sword and a Large Kite Shield (Str 38, Def 46) to protect their large bodies as they do not wear armor like their smaller Mercenary cousins. Giant Skeletons could be found on the grounds surrounding Castle Ehb , and can also be seen lumbering around the grounds of Castle Hiroth . It is unknown if they are actually giants that the Half-Giants descended from or they are Half-Giants' ancestors but shrunken down due to being mixed races. Giant Skeletons are not to be trifled with, thanks to their attack power and high health.

name: Giant Skeleton Stalker
description: Giant Skeleton Stalker Affiliations Mountain of the Dead Regular Damage (as weapon) + 65-81 lvl 10 Armor 165 Experience 388 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 403-631 lvl 52 Armor 165 Experience 46,948 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 637-988 lvl 80 Armor 165 Experience 1,258,284 Giant Skeleton Stalkers are an undead enemy found near the Mountain of the Dead, inside a dungeon connecting it to Jherkal's Crown . They are what appear to be Half-Giant skeletons equipped with a sword and a Dermal Shield, and they accompany Skeletal Archers .

name: Giant Spider
description: Giant Spider Have a picture of Giant Spider? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Giant Spider Fight with the Giant Spider The Giant Spider is a monster encountered in Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna . In both, it serves as a boss battle.

name: Giant Wasp
description: Giant Wasp Affiliations Cliffs of Fire Iliarth Canyon Quillrabe Canyons Regular Damage 90 - 110 Armor 333 Experience 9,000 Veteran Damage 157 - 234 Armor 681 Experience 117,000 Elite Damage 217 - 325 Armor 945 Experience 3,051,000 Giant Wasp Have a picture of Giant Wasp? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Giant Wasp is a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna . This flying, stinging desert insect has an elongated head and dangling legs. The Giant Wasp and Soul Stinger are the strongest winged insects, but as they do not appear to swarm, they are hardly likely to turn battles considering the strength of the other opponents in this environment.

name: Gobbot Blaster
description: Gobbot Blaster Affiliations Goblins Constructs Great Clock Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 21 Armor 257 Experience 2,400 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 124-169 lvl 64 Armor 692 Experience 93,600 Elite Damage (as spell) + 205-279 lvl 92 Armor 965 Experience 2,500,800 The Gobbot Blaster is a version of the normal Blaster tank that is found in the Great Clock Facility.

name: Gobbot General
description: This animatronic leader uses a Gas Gun. It can be obtained from the veteran and elite versions of the creature (by which point it is underpowered) by applying Death Blast and slaying it in melee. Its weapon does not otherwise drop for players.

name: Gobbot Grenadier
description: Keep the party spread-out when facing these in Ehb ; the blast radius is very dangerous when the expansion is not running. As with several other types of armed goblinoids, the first 5 kills on this class of creature can spew its equipped weapon, the Grenade Launcher.

name: Gobbot Gunner
description: This is a goblin-made automaton. As with several other types of armed goblin units, the first 5 kills on this class of creature can spew its equipped weapon, a Dragon Minigun.

name: Gobbot Igniter
description: This is a goblin-made automaton. As with several other types of armed goblin units, the first 5 kills on this class of creature can spew its equipped weapon, a Flamethrower.

name: Gobbot Lobber
description: This is a goblin-made automaton. As with several other types of armed goblin units, the first 5 kills on this class of creature can spew its equipped weapon, a Mini-Grenade Launcher, though this device is not as powerful as the original one used by the Gobbot Grenadier .

name: Gobbot Scorcher
description: Gobbot Scorcher Affiliations Goblins Constructs Great Clock Regular Damage (as weapon) + 68-75 lvl 20 Armor 168 Experience 1,793 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 155-209 lvl 63 Armor 466 Experience 69,927 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 216-291 lvl 91 Armor 650 Experience 1,868,306 The Gobbot Scorcher is a class of Gobbot found in the Great Clock facility. It is essentially a weaker version of the normal Gobbot Igniter and wields a Flameblaster, essentially a junior Flamethrower.

name: Gobbot Shocker
description: This is a goblin-made automaton. As with several other types of armed goblin units, the first 5 kills on this class of creature can spew its equipped weapon, a Lightning Gun.

name: Gobbot Sleeper
description: As with several other types of goblins, the first 5 kills on this class of creature can spew its equipped weapon, a Dragon Minigun.

name: Gobbot Zapper
description: Gobbot Zapper Affiliations Goblins Regular Damage (as weapon) + 68-74 lvl 20 Armor 169 Experience 1,793 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 155-206 lvl 63 Armor 469 Experience 69,927 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 216-287 lvl 91 Armor 654 Experience 1,868,306 Gobbot Zappers are a class of Gobbot employed by the Goblins who inhabit the lands of the Great Clock .

name: Goblin Blackguard
description: Blackguards defend the goblin warrens carved-out around the Great Clock . This class of goblin wears a leather uniform and is armed with a custom-made Goblin Clockwork Hammer (85-95 damage). This weapon can only be obtained on Veteran level or higher, while using the spell Death Blast.

name: Goblin Grunt
description: This is a low-ranked goblin workman. The creature is armed with a Worn Hammer (2-4 damage plus the listed modifiers) better-suited for manufacturing than battle. This type of goblin is unarmored, which suggests that protective gear would be a hindrance to their customary duties in the inventors' workshops.

name: Goblin Guard
description: Goblin guards keep other goblins in line and defend the warrens. The guard wears a thin, wood-and-steel carapace suit and is armed with a Blade (16-29 damage plus the listed modifiers). While not especially tough, the creature can deal considerable damage.

name: Goblin Headsman
description: Goblin headsmen defend the goblin warrens carved-out around the Great Clock . This class of goblin is garbed in scale mail and wields a custom-made Goblin Black Steel (85-91 damage) suited for fast slashing attacks. This weapon can only be obtained on Veteran level or higher, while using the spell Death Blast.

name: Goblin Inventor
description: The Goblin Inventor was a Goblin individual who is presumably responsible for much Goblin technological development in Aranna . He is also responsible for the Seck Resurgence , as he somehow stole the Staff of Stars and imprisoned it's owner, Merik in ice. He guarded Merik's warding staff with a massive battlesuit , though this was destroyed by a party of adventurers who also reclaimed the staff for Merik.

name: Goblin Robo Suit
description: The Robo Suit was designed by the Goblin Inventor . It was a towering, two-legged mech built out of wood and steel and bristling with weaponry. The Inventor would operate the suit from a tiny cockpit in the head and upper torso region. The suit had a shoulder-mounted Lightning Gun, a Grenade Launcher in its center of mass, and a huge Flamethrower and Minigun for arms. The farmgirl and her companions confronted the Inventor in Ehb 's goblin warrens and battled with him for ownership of the Warding Staff . The goblin leader was a skilled pilot, able to keep the mech's legs moving to dodge projectiles while swiveling his weapons to strafe the party. At this point in their quest, though, the party had far more practical combat experience than the goblin leader and set to work targetting weak points in the Robo Suit even under fire. Disabling the mech's weapons one-by-one and finally breaking the central swivel joint, the team forced the Inventor to back off, leaving the staff in their custody and putting a close to Chapter 4: The Warding Staff .

name: Gom
description: Gom Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 85 Armor 780 Experience 170,000 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 106-158 lvl 121 Armor 1,391 Experience 510,000 Elite Damage (as spell) + 203-304 lvl 154 Armor 1,978 Experience 15,810,000 Gom Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 96 Armor 1,370 Experience 300,000 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 106-158 lvl 132 Armor 2,124 Experience 900,000 Elite Damage (as spell) + 203-304 lvl 165 Armor 3,485 Experience 27,900,000 Gom is the god-king of the Seck , and the main antagonist and final boss of Dungeon Siege .

name: Goo Walker
description: These swamp-dwelling zombies pop up from hidden spawn points beneath the ground and chuck viscera at the player via their spell, goo_walker_flesh .

name: Googore
description: Googore Have a picture of Googore? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 0 h2h 160-180 lvl 40 Armor 380 Experience 17,000 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 157-203 lvl 0 h2h 291-400 lvl 82 Armor 787 Experience 170,000 Elite Damage (as spell) + 276-357 lvl 0 h2h 403-555 lvl 114 Armor 1,091 Experience 4,352,000 The Googore is a gigantic cave-dwelling worm-like creature, found in Ehb's Temple Ruins and the Utraean Peninsula's Fury Den .

name: Googore Grub
description: Googore Grub Have a picture of Googore Grub? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Regular Damage h2h 125-135 lvl 17 Armor 190 Experience 2,000 Veteran Damage h2h 227-300 lvl 59 Armor 388 Experience 78,000 Elite Damage h2h 315-416 lvl 91 Armor 538 Experience 2,084,000 Googore grubs are the juvenile version of the Googore , resembling a human-sized worm. They can be found in the Temple Ruins and Fury Den , found respectively in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula .

name: Gorack
description: This humanoid creature may be a primate or, by the look of its face, a relative of the Droog . The Gorack is intelligent, as evidenced by its wearing clothes and leather bands around its gorilla-like arms. It is aggressive, solitary, loud, and most likely carnivorous. It hits very hard in a fight and can take more punishment than other enemies in its habitat regions. It has a miniboss version in the form of the Enraged Gorack .

name: Grave Crawler
description: Grave Crawler Affiliations Undead Wesrin Cross Hovart's Folly Regular Damage h2h 127-140 lvl 12 Armor 170 Experience 2,000 Veteran Damage h2h 317-427 lvl 60 Armor 475 Experience 78,000 Elite Damage h2h 457-616 lvl 93 Armor 685 Experience 2,084,000 Grave Crawlers are very decayed undead corpses that have lost all of their legs except for a single femur, leaving them able to do little else than pathetically crawl toward their targets, thus gaining their name. Compared to other zombies, they are slow but have high health.

name: Gray Wolf
description: Gray Wolf Affiliations Canids Grasslands Regular Damage 8 - 11 Armor 19 Experience 12 Veteran Damage 134 - 172 Armor 508 Experience 13,236 Elite Damage 228 - 268 Armor 819 Experience 537,636 The Gray Wolf is an animal enemy faced in the earlier stages of Dungeon Siege . The other wolves, in ascending order of strength, are:

name: Great Krakbone
description: Great Krakbone The Great Krakbone is a massive, one-eyed monster enemy faced in the flood zone at Xot's Badlands of Legends of Aranna . The creature resembled a green-skinned octopus. It has a large, sharp beak and long tentacles. The Great Krakbone will emerge from the water and swiped at the adventurers with its long tentacles.

name: Great Trog Warrior
description: Great Trog Warrior Have a picture of Great Trog Warrior? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Great Trog Warrior is the miniboss version of the Trog Warrior , boasting higher stats and wearing a red coat of plates.

name: Greater Scrub Scorpion
description: Greater Scrub Scorpion Affiliations Xot's Badlands Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 85-91 lvl 16 Armor 184 Experience 400 Veteran Damage h2h 226-295 lvl 59 Armor 590 Experience 48,400 Elite Damage h2h 319-418 lvl 86 Armor 836 Experience 1,297,200 The Greater Scrub Scorpion is one of two classes of scorpions that can be found in the arid scrub of Xot's Badlands , the other being the Lesser Scrub Scorpion .

name: Green Drake
description: Green Drakes inhabit desert regions, but usually eschew venturing far into the open in favor of their nesting caves... which by virtue of these creatures' presence, are very dangerous places to travel through. Like all drakes, they can utilize claws close-in, or their conic breath weapon at range. The green drake's breath is a luminous, corrosive green mist. Level 86 nature mages can summon these drakes to fight for them on their behalf.

name: Green Shard
description: This is a Shard -type creature of small size encountered in the crystal cave environments. It is stronger than the Teal , stronger but not as hardy as the Blue , and significantly weaker than the Purple . Its spell, shard_zap_green , is a green homing-projectile attack similar to that used by the other shards.

name: Gremal
description: Gremals are a species of long-tusked rodent or lagomorph found in Ehb , hopping around near Stonebridge . They are passive, but have a more dangerous cousin in the form of the Killer Gremal .

name: Gresh
description: Gresh Gresh, standing atop the bones of the Legion's fallen Affiliations Seck Fortress Kroth Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 38 h2h 200-230 lvl 38 Armor 550 Experience 24,000 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 361-637 lvl 79 h2h 355-500 lvl 79 Armor 1,201 Experience 168,000 Elite Damage (as spell) + 651-1149 lvl 112 h2h 497-698 lvl 112 Armor 1,679 Experience 4,656,000 Gresh is a Seck commander who laid siege to Fortress Kroth during the Seck Resurgence . Commander Gresh manipulated the dim-witted Krug into pillaging the farmlands of Ehb , and ordered them to deliver any magical items that they found to him. Given the comments of the Goblin Inventor , the ultimate goal of this was likely to find the Staff of Stars . Using powerful necromancy, the Seck Commander brought a formidable army of the undead to bear against the 10th Legion stationed at Fortress Kroth. Over the course of the next several days, the Second Battle of Fortress Kroth raged, and many Legion soldiers and Droog warriors were lost in the fighting. Eventually, a band of heroes, led by the future Lady Montbarron , challenged Gresh, and slew him before the gates of the fortress.

name: Grizzly Furok
description: Despite its demonic appearance, this creature is based on the furok and is not as dangerous in combat as the more-advanced Furok Slasher until the veteran and elite modes of play. As a miniboss it has better loot probabilities; it will generally always drop a piece of strength-based magic armor or a random magic weapon.

name: Gulik
description: Gulik A Gulik Affiliations Dark Jungle creatures Imps Regular Damage h2h 34-37 lvl 6 Armor 60 Experience 88 Veteran Damage h2h 173-230 lvl 48 Armor 355 Experience 30,712 Elite Damage h2h 268-357 lvl 76 Armor 550 Experience 949,520 The Gulik is a pygmy creature found in the Dark Jungle near Illicor in the Island of the Utraeans .

name: Hassat Archer
description: This is one of several types of ranger Hassat that seem to be based on the cheetah. The Archer carries a Hassat Bow (54-60 dmg).

name: Hassat Caster
description: There several types of spellcaster Hassat which all have distinctive leonine features. To attack, the Caster throws a bright-blue projectile spell oddly labeled as a Fireshot (64-72 dmg).

name: Hassat Commander
description: The Commander resembles a white sabertooth. He carries a Hassat Staff (50-58 dmg), a valid player staff weapon, which he wields one-handed in a skillful display. On the other forearm he bears protective guards fashioned out of Zaurask parts. Because the commanders are encountered relatively early in the campaign, their stats are scaled-down to be lower than the Hassat classes found towards the end. During the fall of the empire, a commander named Kajj was doomed by his Utraean conqueror, post-mortem, to become the Kit of Kajj .

name: Hassat Fighter
description: This sword-wielding Hassat resembles a tiger. Its weapon of choice is a non-dropping Tempered Blade (84-95 dmg). One unique specimen can be found imprisoned in the cell block of Fortress Emarard , though it is unknown if it was incarcerated by Zaurask occupiers or Utraeans . It will attack the player's party if approached or freed.

name: Hassat Hunter
description: This is a low-leveled ranger Hassat. In appearance, the ranger types all seem to be based on a cheetah. His version of Hassat Bow deals 26-36, doubled-up by the extra martial damage given in the infobox.

name: Hassat Mage
description: The Mage is a stronger type of Hassat spellcaster, with lion features and colorful leather pouches. His so-called Fireshot , which is blue, deals 74-80 with penetration determined by his skill level. The orb spell he occasionally refreshes seems more for show, dealing just 7-9 until veteran and elite add stronger bonuses.

name: Hassat Panther
description: Unsurprisingly, the Hassat Panther resembles a panther. His encounter contexts are early; the creature is very resilient, but with a dexterity of only 9, is not overwhelmingly good at beastfu. One unique specimen can be found in the dungeons of Fortress Emarard ; it is unclear if it was imprisoned by the castle's Zaurask occupiers or by Utraeans . It will attack the player's party if approached or freed.

name: Hassat Ranger
description: This is a higher-leveled ranger Hassat, based on a cheetah as the race's other rangers are. He appears to wear somewhat upgraded armor plating. He wields a Hassat Bow (58-66 base dmg).

name: Hassat Runner
description: The Runner is a beastfu user similar to the Hassat Panther , being tough but not immensely dangerous. It resembles a cougar.

name: Hassat Shaman
description: This low level spellcaster Hassat resembles a lion. He is armed with a blue shaman_fireshot projectile spell (35-45 base dmg).

name: Hassat Slicer
description: This creature carries out his stated occupation with a Worn Full Axe (76-82 dmg), which according to the internal comments was probably meant to be a Hassat Moon or other polearm. He resembles a leopard.

name: Headless Corpse
description: Headless Corpse Affiliations Regular Damage h2h 30-52 lvl 8 Armor 87 Experience 138 Veteran Damage h2h 121-257 lvl 50 Armor 403 Experience 40,572 Elite Damage h2h 184-390 lvl 78 Armor 612 Experience 1,087,026 Headless Corpse Affiliations Regular Damage h2h 42-56 lvl 9 Armor 104 Experience 208 Veteran Damage h2h 170-277 lvl 51 Armor 508 Experience 45,344 Elite Damage h2h 258-420 lvl 79 Armor 772 Experience 1,210,560 Headless Corpse Affiliations Regular Damage h2h 30-52 lvl 8 Armor 87 Experience 210 Veteran Damage h2h 121-257 lvl 50 Armor 403 Experience 45,780 Elite Damage h2h 184-390 lvl 78 Armor 612 Experience 1,222,200 The Headless Corpse is a enemy found at Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna It is the weaker cousin to the Headless Zombie found in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula . It can be found all over the Fen of the Frozen and Mountain of the Dead .

name: Headless Zombie
description: Headless Zombie Affiliations Undead Eastern Swamp Lang Mire Regular Damage h2h 75-83 lvl 4 Armor 130 Experience 1,467 Veteran Damage h2h 187-253 lvl 52 Armor 367 Experience 76,284 Elite Damage h2h 270-365 lvl 85 Armor 529 Experience 2,025,927 The Headless Zombie is an undead creature lurking in the swamp and cemeteries. They are beheaded, bloodied corpses that will rise from the ground when sensing adventurers nearby and would attack them immediately. It's unknown how they can see or hear the adventurers without their heads. Despite being slow walkers, but can inflict heavy damages. Level 43 combat mages can summon one to fight on their behalf.

name: Heater
description: This is a treaded robotic tank mounting a flamethrower. It is heavily armored. Its reservoir of combustible fuel is not a weak point in the device and does no harm when the Heater is destroyed. The Heater's flame cannon does the same amount of damage on average as that of the humanoid robot, the Gobbot Igniter . The Heater is built by Goblins for territorial defense. It may serve other uses in their industrial machine - possibly defoliation or metalworking.

name: Hell Boar
description: A heavy, grumbling, four-legged creature that inhabits forests, the Hell Boar bears only a general resemblance to a mammalian boar; on closer inspection it has burnished scales, numerous eye stalks, wormlike mandibles, and a lamprey mouth. The Hell Boar's behavior is nonetheless about as boarish as it gets: it just charges into battle and attempts to maul everything to death. Combat mages of Level 39 or higher can summon one to fight on their behalf. It may be related to the Mhulliqs found on Island of the Utraeans .

name: Horrid
description: Horrid A Horrid attacking a player. Horrid Have a picture of Horrid? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies A Horrid is a monster encountered occasionally in Dungeon Siege .

name: Hunter
description: Hunter Have a picture of Hunter? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Hunters are a class of attack drone fielded by Goblins.

name: Huskar the Shaman
description: Huskar the Shaman Affiliations Krug Stonebridge (south fort) Elddim Glade Fort Regular Damage as weapon as spell + (8 - 12) Armor 70 Experience 85 Veteran Damage as weapon as spell + (82 - 150) Armor 821 Experience 29,665 Elite Damage as weapon as spell + (131 - 240) Armor 1310 Experience 917,150 Huskar the Shaman is a miniboss Krug in Dungeon Siege . He is the strongest magic-user encountered among the Krug and likely the leader of the Shaman caste and their Apprentices .

name: Hydrack
description: The Hydrack is a saurian monster that inhabits swamp lakes. It remains mostly submerged when attacking, so its size and shape are open to speculation, but what does emerge is not very reassuring. The creature has a long and very strong neck many times the size of a human, with dull brown scales patterned much alike to an adder. Its head is draconic, with an elongated mouth, scaled plumage, and horns. The Hydrack attacks exclusively with its breath, which appears to be a cone of poisonous gas or acid. It deals high damage-over-time in a large area in front of the attack trajectory. When killed, Hydrack will rear its head up and roars in agony as its sank back into the depth of the swamp.

name: Ice Archer
description: These elemental creatures are encountered in alpine and ice cavern environments. The archers frequently operate in large packs that can alert one another to approaching foes, and can deliver huge pressure against the player from behind a wall of Ice Warriors . The archers may drop Ice Bows when destroyed.

name: Ice Fly
description: Ice Fly An ice fly. Affiliations Cold locations Elementals Regular Damage h2h 14-22 lvl 3 Armor 78 Experience 115 Veteran Damage h2h 69-133 lvl 57 Armor 574 Experience 33,810 Elite Damage h2h 105-201 lvl 91 Armor 870 Experience 905,855 The Ice Fly is a piddling ice elemental found in deep, magical ice caves throughout Aranna . Locations include Mount Frostspire , the Alpine Caverns , the Glacial Caverns , and Arhok . They are often found in burrows which disintegrate upon total evacuation.

name: Ice Giant
description: Ice Giant Affiliations Arhok Cold Storage Cave Regular Damage ? Armor ? Experience ? Veteran Damage ? Armor ? Experience ? Elite Damage ? Armor ? Experience ? Ice Giant is a towering creature that resembles a giant with pale blue skin, who can be found living inside the frozen caves. They are well muscled beings with white hair. They seems to have sign of intelligence due to wearing clothes.

name: Ice Grub
description: Ice Grub Have a picture of Ice Grub? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Ice Grubs are human-sized worms. They can be found outside of the town of Arhok . Presumably they eventually become Ice Moths , but this isn't represented in-game.

name: Ice Mage
description: The first type hostile human encountered in Ehb and the Peninsula, this creature is a plodding, hunchbacked geriatric with a poor health rating. While he employs an ice blast spell, the chief danger posed by an Ice Mage actually comes from his summoned Ice Warriors , which he can replace indefinitely. Normally his summoning ability makes the Ice Mage a priority target, but players so-inclined can gain experience and treasure off the infinite summons.

name: Ice Moth
description: Ice Moth Affiliations Arhok Cold regions Regular Damage h2h 3-6 lvl 0 Armor 5 Experience 6 Veteran Damage h2h 116-283 lvl 0 Armor 315 Experience 8,088 Elite Damage h2h 191-468 lvl 0 Armor 520 Experience 396,978 Ice Moths are an insect enemy that can be faced early in the game. They can be found outside of the town of Arhok .

name: Ice Warrior
description: These elemental creatures are encountered in alpine and ice cavern environments. They frequently operate in large packs that can alert one another to approaching foes. On many occasions, they may also burst out from stalagmites without warning very close to the party. The warrior groups play linebacker for their Ice Archer kin. They are known to leave behind their valuable Ice Swords when destroyed.

name: Ice Wasp
description: Ice Wasp Affiliations Elementals Regular Damage h2h 18-12 lvl 1 Armor 8 Experience 18 Veteran Damage h2h 243-198 lvl 43 Armor 155 Experience 15,372 Elite Damage h2h 394-321 lvl 71 Armor 251 Experience 494,676 Ice Wasp Have a picture of Ice Wasp? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Ice Wasps are a class of elemental; a weaker version of the Ice Fly found in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula . These flyers are solely found to the south of Arhok , above the Frozen Lake .

name: Immature Chitterskrag
description: Immature Chitterskrag Affiliations Jherkal's Crown Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 47-53 lvl 11 Armor 90 Experience 262 Veteran Damage h2h 153-211 lvl 53 Armor 358 Experience 42,444 Elite Damage h2h 227-313 lvl 81 Armor 532 Experience 1,135,246 The Immature Chitterskrag is the juvenile version of the Chitterskrags native to the Island of the Utraeans .

name: Impaler
description: Impaler Affiliations Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe Canyons Mesa Desert Regular Damage 240 - 260 Armor 440 Experience 46,000 Veteran Damage 413 - 547 Armor 892 Experience 184,000 Elite Damage 566 - 750 Armor 1,223 Experience 4,278,000 Impaler Have a picture of Impaler? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Impaler is a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege . This scorpion-like monster has no frontal claws or pincers, but its long tail can be swung to bludgeon or stab prey. The Impaler emits high-pitched shrieks that do not seem to match its great size. Level 86 combat mages can, given enough mana, summon one to fight on their behalf.

name: Infested Larch
description: Infested Larch Affiliations Regular Damage h2h 175-185 lvl 30 Armor 698 Experience 20,000 Veteran Damage h2h 344-445 lvl 74 Armor 1,563 Experience 140,000 Elite Damage h2h 481-621 lvl 106 Armor 2,180 Experience 3,880,000 The Infested Larch is the miniboss version of the more common Larch . The one in the Utraean Peninsula is found at the end of the Goblin Warrens .

name: Island Lizard
description: Island Lizard Affiliations Jungles Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 22-30 lvl 4 Armor 30 Experience 33 Veteran Damage h2h 158-264 lvl 46 Armor 261 Experience 21,945 Elite Damage h2h 250-418 lvl 74 Armor 413 Experience 608,157 Island Lizards are a species of reptile present on Island of the Utraeans . They resemble a smaller version of the Aranna -native Giant Lizard , but with a distinctive pattern of white stripes on its back. Perhaps the Kraken Shield is made from the skin of this species. It is referred to in the game code as a "bubber".

name: Jade Gargoyle
