description: Jade Gargoyle Affiliations Crypt of the Sacred Blood Ancient Crypt Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 0 Armor 9 Experience 9 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 23-53 lvl 0 Armor 191 Experience 15,669 Elite Damage (as spell) + 45-91 lvl 0 Armor 310 Experience 595,467 Jade Gargoyles are a ranged enemy found in the farmland crypts of Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula.

name: Jungle Angler
description: Jungle Angler Have a picture of Jungle Angler? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Jungle Angler is a monster found in Legends of Aranna at the Island of the Utraeans Southern Coast , along the beaches.

name: Jungle Klaw
description: Jungle Klaw Affiliations Dark Jungle Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 38-41 lvl 6 Armor 45 Experience 132 Veteran Damage h2h 177-234 lvl 48 Armor 247 Experience 38,808 Elite Damage h2h 273-360 lvl 76 Armor 381 Experience 1,039,764 The Jungle Klaw is a variant of the Forest Klaw that is found in the jungles of the Island of the Utraeans . In-game, they are found only in a single pack, and their stats are the lowest of any Klaw in the game.

name: Jungle Phrak
description: Jungle Phrak Regular Damage h2h 30-40 lvl 3 Armor 23 Experience 92 Veteran Damage h2h 168-275 lvl 45 Armor 160 Experience 32,108 Elite Damage h2h 263-429 lvl 73 Armor 250 Experience 992,680 Jungle Phrak Have a picture of Jungle Phrak? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Jungle Phraks are a variety of the Phrak genus ubiquitous throughout Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna . They can be found in the jungly areas of the Island of the Utraeans Southern Coast , emerging from mound-like nests.

name: Jungle Skrubb
description: Jungle Skrubb Affiliations Beaches Jungles Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 4 Armor 35 Experience 83 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 94-146 lvl 46 Armor 301 Experience 28,967 Elite Damage (as spell) + 157-244 lvl 74 Armor 477 Experience 895,570 The Jungle Skrubb is one of the several species of Skrubb present in Legends of Aranna . They can be found in the Island of the Utraeans Southern Coast and the bluffs above it .

name: Jungle Slinger
description: Jungle Slinger Have a picture of Jungle Slinger? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Jungle Slingers are a species of the Lunger genus found around Aranna ; this one in particular can be found in southern part of the Island of the Utraeans . They sling forks at enemies like how their name suggests.

name: Jungle Slinger Camora
description: Jungle Slinger Camora Have a picture of Jungle Slinger Camora? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Jungle Slinger Camora is a unique miniboss version of the Jungle Slinger . Killing it is a step in the quest given by the Utraean merchant Ilorn to retrieve his amulet; it appears that the slinger camora had stolen it.

name: Jungle Spiker
description: Jungle Spiker Have a picture of Jungle Spiker? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Jungle Spiker is a creature found in the jungles of the Island of the Utraeans a weaker analogue to Spiked Maws and Dwellers .

name: Kell
description: Kell Affiliations Enemies Regular Damage as spell + (210 - 250) Armor 510 Experience 100,000 Veteran Damage as spell + (321 - 467) Armor 935 Experience 300,000 Elite Damage as spell + (439 - 638) Armor 1,278 Experience 9,300,000 Kell Have a picture of Kell? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Kell is a hostile monster in Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna . The Kell is a species of serpentine demon often found near volcanic areas. They are pink-skinned, have horned heads with glowing orange eyes and an evil grin of sharp teeth, and they wear nipple-rings on their pectorals. Their tails gradually fade to purple near the end. They are resilient enemies, and are skilled spellcasters. Their spells include a small but painful area-damage-over-time fireball, a tracking globe of magic energy, and bolts of red lightning.

name: Kill Bot
description: Kill Bot Have a picture of Kill Bot? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Kill Bots are a model of attack drone fielded by the Goblins .

name: Killer Gremal
description: Killer Gremal Have a picture of Killer Gremal? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Killer Gremals are a group of over-sized, hairless rodent with long, sharp teeth. These creatures can be found just outside Stonebridge in Ehb and in Hovart Marsh in the Utraean Peninsula . They have pinkish skin and huge red eyes. Despite their plump bodies, they have powerful leg muscles that allows them to hop away from potential threats. These creatures usually can be seen as a large group. When being threatened, they will hop back and forth before using their teeth to chomp on an unlucky adventurer. Nature mages of level 7 or higher can summon a Killer Gremal to fight for them. Gremal are possibly edible, with one of the citizens at Elddim mentioning about cooking one of the creatures for dinner.

name: Killer Vines
description: Killer Vines Have a picture of Killer Vines? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Killer Vines are a group of large sentient carnivorous vines that can be found on the Island of the Utraeans Southern Coast . They emerge from the ground and swipe at the unsuspecting adventurer when being approached. When idling, Killer Vines can be seen swaying the vine around, in hoping to catch their next prey. When being killed, Killer Vines will sink back into the ground.

name: Klandank the Krug
description: Klandank the Krug is very large, as Krug go - fully as tall as, and significantly broader than a human. His skin is a deeper red-orange than ordinary Krug scouts, and he has a glowing green aura. A Krug scoutmaster of sorts, this boss creature leads scouting parties in their accustomed duty of foraging for food and attempting to steal anything they can get their hands on. As with his fellow scouts, Klandank wields a blunt dagger in combat. While he can move with the great speed of a Krug scout, his low damage keeps him from being especially dangerous.

name: Klaw
description: Klaws are tall, hunching creatures with long arms, claws and three large horns crowning their heads. They are common in the snowy forests of Frostspire Mountain in northern Ehb . A variety known as the Forest Klaw can be found within the Dark Forest . Klaws attack with lunging bites by using their gaping mouths, and by slashing with their sharp claws. They usually come in small packs which can be troublesome to some adventurers. Due to its three horns and demonic appearance, it could be possible that the giant carcass found within the Alpine Caverns was a gargantuan Klaw.

name: Krakemaw the Putrid
description: Krakemaw the Putrid Affiliations Lair of Cicatrix Cicatrix Undead Drakes Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 12 h2h 80-90 lvl 12 Armor 142 Experience 388 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 216-297 lvl 54 h2h 288-396 lvl 54 Armor 604 Experience 46,948 Elite Damage (as spell) + 360-495 lvl 82 h2h 432-594 lvl 82 Armor 906 Experience 1,258,284 Krakemaw the Putrid is a miniboss Rahk .

name: Krug Apprentice
description: Krug Apprentice Affiliations Krug Farmlands Regular Damage as spell + (4 - 7) Armor 12 Experience 12 Veteran Damage as spell + (294 - 315) Armor 920 Experience 13,236 Elite Damage as spell + (320 - 685) Armor 1486 Experience 537,636 The Krug Apprentice is a type of Krug in Dungeon Siege .

name: Krug Chucker
description: Krug Chucker Affiliations Krug Glitterdelve territory Crystwind territory Regular Damage as weapon + (7 - 9) Armor 22 Experience 27 Veteran Damage as weapon + (180 - 260) Armor 276 Experience 22,005 Elite Damage as weapon + (287 - 413) Armor 470 Experience 742,014 The Krug Chucker is a class of Krug in Dungeon Siege .

name: Krug Commander
description: Krug Commander Affiliations Krug Great Northern Forest Mines Regular Damage as weapon + (30 - 34) Armor 86 Experience 151 Veteran Damage as weapon + (326 - 487) Armor 594 Experience 44,394 Elite Damage as weapon + (521 - 773) Armor 908 Experience 1,189,427 The Krug Commander is a type of Krug in Dungeon Siege .

name: Krug Dog
description: Krug Dogs have a roughly dog-like appearance and cream-white pelt. They have a stronger attack than the Krug Scavengers and Scouts which are often in their vicinity, thanks to their massive, dagger-like canines. The Dogs can be found in large packs in the Mine settings, but will no longer pose much of a threat at this stage.

name: Krug Grunt
description: Krug Grunt Affiliations Krug Glitterdelve territory Crystwind territory Regular Damage as weapon + (15 - 17) Armor 30 Experience 19 Veteran Damage as weapon + (161 - 204) Armor 345 Experience 16,226 Elite Damage as weapon + (251 - 330) Armor 582 Experience 622,158 The Krug Grunt is a type of common Krug in Dungeon Siege .

name: Krug Guard
description: As an opening salvo in the campaigns on Ehb and the Peninsula , the Krug attacked farmlands and mines en masse . Krug Guards were the toughest regulars seen during the event. Their job was evidently to hold captured territory, keep the other krug in line, and carry out direct orders from leaders. Whenever guards were encountered, it was a sure bet that a Commander would also be nearby. Krug Guards resembled Raiders but were nearly twice as strong. They wore a mishmash of armor plates around the shoulders, legs, and waist. Their red waistbands and the embossed skull on their chestplates could have been a mark of rank or an attempt at a uniform within the Krug military machine. Large congregations of krug guards gave battle in the Glitterdelve Mines and under Crystwind . Another large force established a warcamp in the Great Northern Forest , with a particularly high concentration of commanders there driving the krug south into the farmlands surrounding Elddim .

name: Krug Killer Dog
description: More dangerous and far less common than ordinary Krug Dogs , these creatures accompany Krug Raiders , Guards , and Commanders .

name: Krug Raider
description: Krug Raider Affiliations Krug Glitterdelve Mine Crystwind Mine Regular Damage as weapon + (12 - 16) Armor 42 Experience 36 Veteran Damage as weapon + (262 - 411) Armor 527 Experience 23,940 Elite Damage as weapon + (411 - 643) Armor 812 Experience 663,444 The Krug Raider is a higher-ranked Krug found in Dungeon Siege .

name: Krug Scavenger
description: Krug Scavenger Affiliations Krug Farmlands Regular Damage as weapon + (1 - 2) Armor 2 Experience 4 Veteran Damage as weapon + (127 - 164) Armor 247 Experience 12,172 Elite Damage as weapon + (200 - 280) Armor 401 Experience 422,840 The Krug Scavenger is a type of Krug enemy in Dungeon Siege .

name: Krug Scout
description: Krug Scout Affiliations Krug Farmlands Great Northern Forest Regular Damage as weapon + (2 - 3) Armor 9 Experience 6 Veteran Damage as weapon + (143 - 231) Armor 658 Experience 9088 Elite Damage as weapon + (260 - 350) Armor 1019 Experience 396,978 The Krug Scout is a type of Krug in Dungeon Siege .

name: Krug Shaman
description: Krug Shaman Affiliations Krug Glitterdelve territory Crystwind territory Regular Damage as spell + (8 - 12) Armor 22 Experience 16 Veteran Damage as spell + (174 - 216) Armor 530 Experience 17,648 Elite Damage as spell + (306 - 342) Armor 908 Experience 716,848 The Krug Shaman is a type of Krug in Dungeon Siege .

name: Larch
description: Larch Affiliations Hostile wildlife Redwood Gap Bonepicker's Trading Post Regular Damage h2h 175-185 lvl 20 Armor 480 Experience 10,000 Veteran Damage h2h 344-445 lvl 64 Armor 1,057 Experience 130,000 Elite Damage h2h 481-621 lvl 96 Armor 1,475 Experience 3,390,000 Larches are an enemy found near the Temple Ruins and in Redwood Gap, respectively in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula. They resembles the barkrunner and bog beast , and they usually punch the adventurers with their beefy fists. It is unknown whether or not these large beasts are based on actual larch trees.

name: Lava Beast
description: Lava Beast Affiliations Elementals Vault of Eternity Volcanic Caverns Regular Damage as spell + (190 - 220) Armor 800 Experience 130,000 Veteran Damage as spell + (290 - 410) Armor 1451 Experience 390,000 Elite Damage as spell + (396 - 561) Armor 1983 Experience 12,090,000 The Lava Beast is an enemy in Dungeon Siege . This creature has a miniboss version, the Magma Beast .

name: Lava Horror
description: Lava Horror Affiliations Imps Regular Damage 160 - 200 Armor 550 Experience 100,000 Veteran Damage 243 - 371 Armor 1,001 Experience 300,000 Elite Damage 331 - 505 Armor 1,364 Experience 9,300,000 The Lava Horror is a hostile class of Imp in Dungeon Siege . The Lava horror is a melee fighter in the ranks of volcanic impkind. They are small, red-skinned, well-muscled, have huge, glowing yellow compound eyes, are covered in all sorts of demonic horns, and they wear green underpants.

name: Lava Imp
description: Lava Imp Affiliations Imps Regular Damage 160 - 200 Armor 500 Experience 87,000 Veteran Damage 249 - 381 Armor 917 Experience 261,000 Elite Damage 338 - 516 Armor 1,244 Experience 8,091,000 The Lava Imp is a hostile class of Imp in Dungeon Siege . Lava imps are a melee enemy found in the end-game regions. They are short, mauve-skinned, horned, and evil beings, eager to attack anyone who enters their territory. It is unknown how this creature, or any of its related kin, came into being. They are most commonly found in volcanic areas and Utraean ruins.

name: Lava Mage
description: Lava Mage Affiliations Imps Regular Damage single-shot spell + (160 - 200) Armor 523 Experience 100,000 Veteran Damage single-shot spell + (245 - 374) Armor 963 Experience 300,000 Elite Damage single-shot spell + (334 - 511) Armor 1,316 Experience 9,300,000 The Lava Mage is a hostile class of Imp in Dungeon Siege . Lava mages are ranged attackers in the ranks of volcanic impkind. They are gray-skinned, have glowing yellow eyes, and have elaborately-shaped horns. They cast a highly-damaging but slow-moving orb of magical energy at their targets.

name: Lava Runner
description: Lava Runner Affiliations Imps Regular Damage dual-shot spell + (170 - 190) Armor 500 Experience 87,000 Veteran Damage dual-shot spell + (265 - 362) Armor 929 Experience 261,000 Elite Damage dual-shot spell + (359 - 490) Armor 1,260 Experience 8,091,000 The Lava Runner is a hostile class of Imp in Dungeon Siege . Lava runners are ranged mages in the ranks of volcanic impkind. They have large, glowing yellow eyes and red horns protruding from their temples and joints, and they are covered in mummy-like linen wrapping. They often come in large numbers, and they cast two damaging magic orbs at a moderate speed.

name: Lava Spirit
description: An elemental being born from magma, the Lava Spirit normally idles on the ground as a pile of glowing volcanic rocks. When the player enters its range, the rocks coalesce into a two-armed creature that can levitate, albeit very slowly, in the player's direction. The Lava Spirit has a piercing damage boost over-and-above its listed damage rating. If it is allowed to close in, it can deal traumatic punishment with its melee attack. Don't let its slow speed lull you into a sense of curiosity. The Kingdom of Ehb variant, Lava Spirit (Magic) , possesses a ranged attack.

name: Lectar
description: Lectar Affiliations Subterranean River Crystal Caverns Sulfur Tunnels Regular Damage h2h 68-74 lvl 15 Armor 120 Experience 683 Veteran Damage h2h 238-316 lvl 67 Armor 515 Experience 62,153 Elite Damage h2h 353-470 lvl 101 Armor 765 Experience 1,663,105 The Lectar is a comparatively-small reptilian monster that appears in Dungeon Siege. They look like a brown lizard with the eyes of a hammerhead shark and the mouth of a scorpion.

name: Leetch
description: Leetch Affiliations Fen of the Frozen Regular Damage h2h 45-55 lvl 8 Armor 60 Experience 120 Veteran Damage h2h 182-272 lvl 50 Armor 283 Experience 35,280 Elite Damage h2h 276-413 lvl 78 Armor 430 Experience 945,240 Leetches are an invertebrate species found in the Fen of the Frozen . They closely resemble Slargs .

name: Lesser Krakbone
description: Lesser Krakbone The Lesser Krakbone is a miniature, one-eyed monster that dwells faced in the flood zone at Xot’s Badlands of Dungeon Siege . The creature resembled a green-skinned octopus, about a size of the player. It has a large, sharp beak and long tentacles.

name: Lesser Scrub Scorpion
description: Lesser Scrub Scorpion Affiliations Xot's Badlands Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 72-81 lvl 15 Armor 121 Experience 573 Veteran Damage h2h 191-263 lvl 58 Armor 394 Experience 52,143 Elite Damage h2h 271-372 lvl 85 Armor 558 Experience 1,395,255 The Lesser Scrub Scorpion is one of two classes of scorpions that can be found in the arid scrub of Xot's Badlands, the other being the Greater Scrub Scorpion .

name: Lord Hovart
description: Lord Hovart Lord Hovart, risen into terrible undeath Lord Hovart is an enemy encountered within Hovart's Folly , in the Utraean Peninsula . He appears as part of the quest, Hovart's Folly .

name: Lost Queen
description: Lost Queen Affiliations The Lost Queen Twisted Mucosa Raiders Regular Damage h2h lvl 0 Armor 35 Experience 69 Veteran Damage h2h lvl 0 Armor 774 Experience 32,775 Elite Damage h2h lvl 0 Armor 1,216 Experience 893,205

name: Magma Beast

name: Maljin Assassin
description: Maljin Assassin Affiliations Maljin Utraean Catacombs Regular Damage h2h 190-250 lvl 35 Armor 760 Experience 87,000 Veteran Damage h2h 282-400 lvl 71 Armor 1,446 Experience 261,000 Elite Damage h2h 385-545 lvl 104 Armor 1,970 Experience 8,091,000 The Maljin Assassin is what could be considered the "final boss" of the main questline of the Utraean Peninsula , found as a miniboss guarding the Tenstone platform in the Utraean Catacombs. It has a slightly-modified Phantom body, but fights in melee like a beefed-up Stalker .

name: Maljin Jolt
description: Maljin Jolt Affiliations Maljin Utraean Catacombs Volcanic Caverns Utraean Peninsula Regular Damage as spell + (150 - 180) Armor 440 Experience 87,000 Veteran Damage as spell + (223 - 328) Armor 951 Experience 261,000 Elite Damage as spell + (304 - 446) Armor 1,296 Experience 8,091,000 The Maljin Jolt is a hostile class of Maljin in Dungeon Siege . This diminutive, grey-skinned demon makes a persistent and very rapid attack with small, obsidian projectiles. The Jolt has the weakest attack out of the three main classes of maljin, but it is still a considerable source of pressure in battle.

name: Maljin Phantom
description: Maljin Phantom Affiliations Maljin Utraean Catacombs Volcanic Caverns Utraean Peninsula Regular Damage as spell + (140 - 180) Armor 370 Experience 87,000 Veteran Damage as spell + (209 - 328) Armor 772 Experience 261,000 Elite Damage as spell + (284 - 446) Armor 1,052 Experience 8,091,000 The Maljin Phantom is a hostile class of Maljin in Dungeon Siege . This is a small, red-orange-skinned demon with elaborate horns. It utilizes a quick, flashy rotation of spells, including an orange fireball, an orb of purple eldritch energy, and a cluster of green homing missiles, that are very difficult to avoid and inflict serious pain.

name: Maljin Stalker
description: Maljin Stalker Affiliations Maljin Utraean Catacombs Volcanic Caverns Utraean Peninsula Regular Damage 160 - 215 Armor 650 Experience 87,000 Veteran Damage 238 - 364 Armor 1,253 Experience 261,000 Elite Damage 325 - 496 Armor 1,707 Experience 8,091,000 The Maljin Stalker is a hostile class of Maljin in Dungeon Siege . This is a small and wiry demon covered in white sarashi wrappings. Apparently natural martial artists, Maljin Stalkers are very fast and dangerous melee opponents.

name: Mangler
description: A Mangler is a highly-aggressive amphibian monster. They take the general shape of a deep-sea fish, and have a white chitinous exterior, huge maw and gullet, fins to allow motion underwater, and a single eye. They are also equipped with clawed forearms that allow them to quickly crawl overland in pursuit of food. These creatures are encountered in groups. While they seem to enjoy basking on open ground, they will also lie concealed in the muck, watching for prey.

name: Mantrap
description: Mantrap Have a picture of Mantrap? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Hostile wildlife Fen of the Frozen Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 8 h2h 47-55 lvl 9 Armor 107 Experience 184 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 126-193 lvl 50 h2h 190-272 lvl 51 Armor 493 Experience 40,112 Elite Damage (as spell) + 210-321 lvl 78 h2h 289-413 lvl 79 Armor 749 Experience 1,070,880 Mantraps are an enemy found in the Fen of the Frozen . They resemble a Mine Worm with the texture of a cucumber.

name: Marble Golem
description: Marble Golem Have a picture of Marble Golem? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Marble Golems are golems that made of stone that guards the Utraean Ruins. They crushes anyone with their arms to those who they sees as a threat. At the height of the Utraeans powers, no luxury was too small, since the supply of slave labor and resources seemed limitless. Golems were originally created as heavy lifters for the Utraean industry. In the ultra posh Transportation Center, they were even created out of the same marble quarried for the walls and floors of the building, so as not to offend the delicate aesthetic sensibilities of the patrons. After hundreds of years of neglect and abandonment, however, their programming has gone somewhat haywire, and the previously docile and subservient creatures now attack just about anything that comes nearby. Their stone structure and industrial strength muscles make them fearsome opponents.

name: Mechanized Wasp
description: Mechanized Wasp Affiliations Goblins Great Clock Regular Damage h2h 87-95 lvl 21 Armor 137 Experience 1,195 Veteran Damage h2h 203-270 lvl 64 Armor 433 Experience 62,140 Elite Damage h2h 284-379 lvl 92 Armor 606 Experience 1,650,295 Mechanized wasps are robot versions of the Giant Wasp that can be found in the Great Clock facility.

name: Mhulliq
description: Mhulliq Affiliations Utrae Southern Coast Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 60-70 lvl 4 Armor 29 Experience 59 Veteran Damage h2h 378-539 lvl 46 Armor 221 Experience 28,025 Elite Damage h2h 594-847 lvl 74 Armor 348 Experience 763,755 Mhulliqs are a creature found in the jungly areas of Island of the Utraeans . They resemble Hell Boars but have higher pitched voices. They have a stronger cousin in the form of the Rending Mhulliq .

name: Mine Worm
description: Mine Worm A MineWorm The MineWorm is a creature faced exclusively in the Glitterdelve Mine and the Crystwind mines .

name: Molten Drake
description: Molten Drake Affiliations Drakes Vault of Eternity Volcanic Caverns Regular Damage claw & breath + (220 - 250) Armor 680 Experience 170,000 Veteran Damage claw & breath + (328 - 455) Armor 1,337 Experience 510,000 Elite Damage claw & breath + (446 - 620) Armor 1,822 Experience 15,810,000 The Molten Drake is a hostile class of Drake in Dungeon Siege . Molten Drakes have resplendent orange scales, supposedly from a super-heated body core. They attack foes either with their cone of flaming breath or their powerful claws. The Molten and Black are the top drake breeds; the former is tougher and the latter hits harder. Their experience value is enormous.

name: Molten Steel Drake
description: The Molten Steel Drake has uniquely-brilliant, red-orange markings and a glowing aura of power distinguishing it from its minion drakes. Besides Gom , this monster has the highest experience value of all foes in Dungeon Siege . The Steel drake in Ehb is located in the depths of the Vault of Eternity and, in fact, not far at all before Gom. The one on the Utraean Peninsula is at the end of a series of nearly-immersed rock paths in the northwest end of the Volcanic Caverns, the side accessed from the Lost Pyramid of the Dead.

name: Monstrous Rat
description: Monstrous Rat Affiliations Rats Mines Regular Damage h2h 49-55 lvl 12 Armor 86 Experience 302 Veteran Damage h2h 153-210 lvl 54 Armor 329 Experience 48,924 Elite Damage h2h 225-309 lvl 82 Armor 486 Experience 1,308,566 Monstrous Rat Have a picture of Monstrous Rat? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Monstrous Rat is a unique rodent Boss found at Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna . It can be encountered in the Glitterdelve Mines and the Elddim side of the Crystwind Mines . Another one can be found in the Waystation in the Island of the Utraeans Southern Coast . With lurid green eyes and what looks like exposed flesh along its back, the Monstrous Rat is a unique enemy amongst its giant rat counterparts.

name: Mountain Phrak
description: Mountain Phrak Affiliations Mount Kreth Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 104-120 lvl 19 Armor 132 Experience 990 Veteran Damage h2h 248-350 lvl 62 Armor 392 Experience 68,310 Elite Damage h2h 349-492 lvl 90 Armor 552 Experience 1,813,680 Mountain Phraks are a species of Phrak that can be found in nests on the polluted slopes of Mt. Kreth, where the Great Clock is located.

name: Mountain Quadscale
description: Mountain Quadscale Affiliations Hostile Wildlife, Ramparts Regular Damage h2h 95-104 lvl 16 Armor 180 Experience 1,044 Veteran Damage h2h 245-328 lvl 59 Armor 552 Experience 72,036 Elite Damage h2h 349-467 lvl 87 Armor 786 Experience 1,912,608 The Mountain Quadscale is a weaker cousin of the Quadscales found in Aranna's volcanic areas.

name: Mountain Troll
description: The Mountain Troll is a large, scrawny ape-like creature with green fur. They have yellow eyes, small pointy ears and have unusually long forearms. They can be encountered at Mount Kreth, alongside with the Mutant Goblin Warrior, Mutant Goblin Hurler , Troll Masher , and Mountain Phrak . The Mountain Trolls work together as a huge pack and use that tactic to overwhelm any adventurers.

name: Mucosa
description: Mucosa Affiliations Mucosae Regular Damage 18 - 22 Armor 30 Experience 36 Veteran Damage 132 - 212 Armor 325 Experience 23,940 Elite Damage 214 - 319 Armor 566 Experience 663,444 Mucosa Have a picture of Mucosa? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Mucosa is a class of enemy creature in Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna . Stats given in the panel are for the eponymous creature seen early-on.

name: Mucosa Brute
description: This early-game foe is the only bonafied "miniboss" found among this spider race despite the many kinds of Mucosae that exist.

name: Mucosa Predator
description: Mucosa Predator Affiliations Mucosae Regular Damage as spell + (180 - 220) Armor 520 Experience 50,000 Veteran Damage as spell + (283 - 422) Armor 978 Experience 200,000 Elite Damage as spell + (388 - 579) Armor 1,341 Experience 4,650,000 The Mucosa Predator is a hostile class of Mucosa in Dungeon Siege . This type of mucosa has an unmistakable, luminous blue-and-purple carapace. The Predator is a ranged attacker that spits high-damage purple slime projectiles, and is often seen marauding alongside Mucosa Raiders and other mindlessly-destructive monsters. Given enough mana, level 80 combat mages can summon them to fight on their behalf.

name: Mucosa Raider
description: Mucosa Raider Affiliations Mucosae Seck Maljin Regular Damage 185 - 205 Armor 430 Experience 55,000 Veteran Damage 299 - 405 Armor 864 Experience 165,000 Elite Damage 411 - 557 Armor 1,188 Experience 5,115,000 The Mucosa Raider is a hostile class of Mucosa in Dungeon Siege . This is a physically large, melee-type mucosa with dull yellow-and-green markings. It probably derives its name from a natural inclination to run in the company of other rampaging monsters, including the Mucosa Predator , wreaking havoc.

name: Mummy
description: Mummy Affiliations Undead Mountain of the Dead Lair of Cicatrix Regular Damage h2h 58-67 lvl 10 Armor 115 Experience 237 Veteran Damage h2h 208-294 lvl 52 Armor 472 Experience 38,394 Elite Damage h2h 313-442 lvl 80 Armor 708 Experience 1,026,921 Mummies are an undead foe found in and around the Mountain of the Dead in the Island of the Utraeans . They are usually found in refuse piles inside Cicatrix 's dungeons.

name: Muong Troll
description: A miniboss version of the Forest Troll , the Muong Troll is supposedly an opportunist that has immigrated from the mountains and established dominance in the forest. It is distinguished by a glowing aura of nature energy and has a scaly skin texture very different from Forest Trolls. The Muong Troll in Ehb is in the Dark Forest just beyond the Traveler Camp . The one on the Peninsula is not too far outside Meren in the Cloud Forest.

name: Mutant Goblin Hurler
description: This class of goblin is seen wandering around in the acid rain-ridden wastelands, on the fringes of Island of the Utraeans ' s goblin society. Together with the mutated Warriors, they are used as meat shields to protect the brainier folk living within the Great Clock facility. Not surprisingly, the Hurlers are adept at hurling rocks for a fast, powerful ranged attack. Unlike the rest of the Great Clock Goblins, this class does not drop its weapon, even when killed while Death Blast is in effect. The creature wears a leather kikko vest and resembles a very sickly-colored Krug (having the same mesh).

name: Mutated Goblin Warrior
description: This class of goblin is seen wandering around in the acid rain-ridden wastelands, on the fringes of Island of the Utraeans ' s goblin society. They are used as meat shields to protect the brainier folk living within the Great Clock facility. They bring about a slow, very powerful attack with their Cutter-Hammers. This weapon can only be obtained on Veteran level or higher, while using the spell Death Blast. The mutants have the base mesh of Krug . They emit wimpy little squeals when attacked or slain— what their toxin exposure added to their muscle mass, it might perhaps have subtracted from other places.

name: Noctiss
description: Noctiss Have a picture of Noctiss? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Noctiss are ghostly creatures that can be found in the ruins of the Tower of Kmethekt . They appear to wear a black robe that ends with a short "tail" at the end. These ghostly creatures vanish in a puff of smoke while letting out an unearthly moan when being killed. Like other ghosts, they attack using a spell that resembles Leech Life, but does not actually replenish their health. There are two variants; one casts a green spell, and the other casts red.

name: Nosirrom
description: Nosirrom "The child has defeated me!" Nosirrom was the king of the Zaurask , until his defeat at the hands of the Hero of Arhok . He lead the Zaurask in their genocidal war against the Utraeans and the Hassat , and eventually acquired the technology that accelerated his evolution.

name: Octodrak
description: Octodrak Affiliations Tower of Kmethekt Regular Damage h2h 64-70 lvl 13 Armor 148 Experience 623 Veteran Damage h2h 184-247 lvl 55 Armor 515 Experience 56,693 Elite Damage h2h 269-360 lvl 83 Armor 751 Experience 1,517,005 Octodraks are monsters found in the sunken ruins of Kmethekt. They appear to be tentacle-like enemies that spring up from the ground and attack, much like the Unguis of Aranna .

name: Perforator
description: The Perforator is a Goblin war machine, meant to provide light fire support to infantry by means of dual rotary cannons. It is armored with wood and steel, and it walks on four legs. The machine is powered by a large battery mounted on its back.

name: Phrak
description: Phrak Affiliations Krug Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 4-6 lvl 0 Armor 1 Experience 3 Veteran Damage h2h 150-195 lvl 0 Armor 540 Experience 3,129 Elite Damage h2h 250-330 lvl 88 Armor 890 Experience 242,130 The Phrak appear exclusively in Dungeon Siege . They are referenced twice in Dungeon Siege III but they are never seen. Phrak are small flying green insects that closely resemble bees. They have two large eyes and two large wings upon their upper body and their wings allow them to hover above ground at around the players head height. By themselves, Phrak present no issue to the player as they have minimal attacking ability and also have low health.

name: Phrak Ballista
description: Phrak Ballista Affiliations Phraks Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 0 Armor 15 Experience 10 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 143-202 lvl 0 Armor 1,297 Experience 11,030 Elite Damage (as spell) + 240-320 lvl 0 Armor 1,939 Experience 448,030 The Phrak Ballista is a miniboss version of the Phrak Piercer . One is found on the road to Stonebridge in Ehb , and the other in Iliarth Divide in the Utraean Peninsula .

name: Phrak Piercer
description: Phrak Piercer Affiliations Phraks Krug Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 0 Armor 1 Experience 4 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 122-162 lvl 0 Armor 540 Experience 4,172 Elite Damage (as spell) + 184-284 lvl 0 Armor 890 Experience 322,840 Phrak Piercers are a ranged class of Phrak. Blue in color, they are the first ranged enemy to be encountered in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula . They are blue in color and launch an arrow-like spine at targets. They are the second-weakest foe in Dungeon Siege, just above the Yellow Spider in health.

name: Picker
description: Picker Affiliations Regular Damage h2h 119-125 lvl 22 Armor 315 Experience 10,600 Veteran Damage h2h 228-293 lvl 65 Armor 742 Experience 137,800 Elite Damage h2h 321-412 lvl 98 Armor 1,045 Experience 3,593,400 Picker Have a picture of Picker? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Pickers are a dark-coloured, spider-like creature that live in the water dungeons of Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula , they are also featured at both Dungeon Siege and Legends of Aranna . Like in the Flooded Sanctuary . A weaker variety can also be found in the Halls of the Lost . They have small eyes and a big jaw, with four long legs. The Kill Bot and the Hunter , two Goblin combat drones, appear to be based on the Picker. Level 48 combat mages can summon them to fight on their behalf.

name: Pitiful Braak
description: This miniboss creature seems to have discarded the clothing of his peers in favor of glowing blue markings. What he has done to earn the descriptor "pitiful" is unclear. The creature will always drop a piece of strength-based magic armor or a random magic weapon when slain.

name: Possessed Armor
description: Possessed Armor Have a picture of Possessed Armor? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Possessed Armor is a translucent green-colored shade that wears a set of plate armor. The shade runs slightly slower than an adventurer but is durable and can inflict devastating damage with its punches. Many were found in the Lair of Cicatrix . Combat mages of skill level 36 or higher can summon one of their own to assist in fighting.

name: Possessed Skull
description: Possessed Skull Have a picture of Possessed Skull? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 33 Armor 380 Experience 23,000 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 129-177 lvl 74 Armor 766 Experience 161,000 Elite Damage (as spell) + 232-320 lvl 107 Armor 1,068 Experience 4,462,000 Possessed Skull Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 11 Armor 94 Experience 311 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 173-257 lvl 53 Armor 388 Experience 50,382 Elite Damage (as spell) + 288-429 lvl 81 Armor 582 Experience 1,347,563 Possessed Skulls are a member of a family of identical-looking hostiles, a giant fleshy demonic skull that casts a flaming skull at its target. There are two varieties of Possessed Skull; one can be found in Ehb, in the Hall of Skulls . The other weaker variant can be found in the Lair of Cicatrix .

name: Proxo
description: Proxo Proxo detonating The Proxo is a self-destructing war construct.

name: Punisher
description: Punisher Affiliations Undead Regular Damage as spell + (260 - 320) Armor 210 Experience 40,000 Veteran Damage as spell + (420 - 632) Armor 425 Experience 160,000 Elite Damage as spell + (577 - 869) Armor 584 Experience 3,720,000 The Punisher is an undead monster in Dungeon Siege . There are other giant skulls encountered before this type; they include:

name: Purple Shard
description: This is the biggest and strongest Shard -type creature encountered in the crystal cave environments— more dangerous than the Teal , Green , or Blue . Its spell, shard_zap_purple , is a homing-projectile attack similar to that used by other shards. It has a fairly high inherent damage factor (82-88) in excess of the listed values that makes it a serious threat on the Normal, non-Vet/Elite mode.

name: Quadscale
description: Quadscale Affiliations Vault of Eternity Volcanic Caverns Regular Damage 160 - 180 Armor 420 Experience 100,000 Veteran Damage 238 - 328 Armor 808 Experience 300,000 Elite Damage 325 - 446 Armor 1,101 Experience 9,300,000 The Quadscale is a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege . Quadscales are four-legged, armored insectoid creatures often found in volcanic areas. They possess a very painful bite, but they move slowly. Some weaker individuals were found near Fortress Emarard .

name: Quillrabe Canyons
description: The Quillrabe Canyons are a series of canyons cut by the Iliarth River into the scorching hardpan deserts of the southern Utraean Peninsula . Within the canyons lie the eponymous town of Quillrabe , center of Droog activity in the Peninsula. The landscape is treacherous; home to many deadly creatures, ranging from ferocious demonic imps to gigantic insectoid creatures. In addition, at the time of the Reunification of the Townstones , they were crawling with Droog ambush parties.

name: Rabid Spiked Maw
description: Rabid Spiked Maw Affiliations Hostile wildlife Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 0 Armor 241 Experience 998 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 172-224 lvl 0 Armor 899 Experience 68,862 Elite Damage (as spell) + 284-370 lvl 0 Armor 1,318 Experience 1,828,336 The Rabid Spiked Maw is the miniboss version of the normal Spiked Maw .

name: Rahk
description: Rahk Affiliations Lair of Cicatrix Cicatrix Undead Drakes Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 12 h2h 80-90 lvl 12 Armor 150 Experience 388 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 216-297 lvl 54 h2h 288-396 lvl 54 Armor 636 Experience 46,948 Elite Damage (as spell) + 360-495 lvl 82 h2h 432-594 lvl 82 Armor 954 Experience 1,258,284 Rahks are undead drakes that inhabit the Lair of Cicatrix . They are mostly white with a green belly. They have a stronger counterpart in the Scaly Rahk , and a miniboss in Krakemaw the Putrid .

name: Reaper
description: Reaper Affiliations Cicatrix Skeletons Regular Damage (as weapon) + 50-58 lvl 10 Armor 120 Experience 213 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 381-554 lvl 52 Armor 520 Experience 46,434 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 610-877 lvl 80 Armor 785 Experience 1,239,660 Reapers are special skeletal bodyguards summoned by Cicatrix to help defend him.

name: Rector
description: Rector Affiliations Wesrin Cross Hovart's Folly Undead Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 4 Armor 25 Experience 44 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 138-218 lvl 58 Armor 256 Experience 29,260 Elite Damage (as spell) + 227-359 lvl 93 Armor 406 Experience 810,876 A Rector is a class of Undead foe in Dungeon Siege . The eponymous Rector is the basic version of this creature; the others are:

name: Red Drake
description: Red Drake Affiliations Drakes Abandoned Ruins Vault of Eternity Volcanic Caverns Regular Damage claw & breath + (200 - 250) Armor 560 Experience 150,000 Veteran Damage claw & breath + (303 - 463) Armor 1,177 Experience 450,000 Elite Damage claw & breath + (413 - 632) Armor 1,604 Experience 13,950,000 The Red Drake is a hostile class of Drake in Dungeon Siege . The Red variant is physically larger than other drakes and has dull brick-colored scales. Despite its size, it is not quite as powerful as Black or Molten Drakes.

name: Rending Mhulliq
description: Rending Mhulliq Affiliations Jherkal's Crown Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 45-55 lvl 12 Armor 95 Experience 238 Veteran Damage h2h 153-229 lvl 54 Armor 408 Experience 38,556 Elite Damage h2h 229-342 lvl 82 Armor 609 Experience 1,031,254 The Rending Mhulliq , found in the jungle around Jherkal's Crown , is a greater cousin of the normal Mhulliq .

name: Rock Beast
description: Rock Beast Affiliations Elementals Mines Regular Damage as spell + (65 - 69) Armor 115 Experience 113 Veteran Damage as spell + (298 - 428) Armor 743 Experience 33,222 Elite Damage as spell + (487 - 626) Armor 1144 Experience 890,101 Rock Beast Have a picture of Rock Beast? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Rock Beast is an enemy creature in Dungeon Siege .

name: Rogue Bandit
description: The Rogue Bandit is a swordsman with unusual skills and attire, as bandits go. He deals around twice the damage of an ordinary melee mook, and with his superior armor rating, can take about twice the punishment. He wears a form-fitting suit of chains and rivets suspiciously similar to that of a punk-rocker, and like other bandits in his organization he has opted to shave his head and don a face mask. His weapon of choice is a Full Gladius. The Rogue appears to have a leadership position among otherwise-lawless men; therefore, he must be somewhat tougher and smarter than those around him, and able to count on allies for assistance.

name: Rotten Troll
description: Rotten Troll Affiliations Eastern Swamp Mount Utrae North Slope Trolls Regular Damage (as weapon) + 27-32 lvl 16 Armor 384 Experience 4,250 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 174-264 lvl 64 Armor 1,123 Experience 93,500 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 282-416 lvl 97 Armor 1,611 Experience 2,524,500 The Rotten Troll is the miniboss version of the Swamp Troll .

name: Ruby Gargoyle
description: Ruby Gargoyle Have a picture of Ruby Gargoyle? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Ruby Gargoyle is an animated boss enemy in ' Dungeon Siege .

name: Rune Beast
description: Rune Beast Affiliations Rune Casters Demlock's Ravine Regular Damage h2h 60-66 lvl 13 Armor 138 Experience 520 Veteran Damage h2h 180-242 lvl 55 Armor 490 Experience 47,320 Elite Damage h2h 264-354 lvl 83 Armor 718 Experience 1,266,200 The Rune Beasts are melee-fighting rune minotaur-like rune monsters. They come in a richer shade of tan than their Fiend counterparts, and can be either found roaming or summoned.

name: Rune Caster
description: Rune Caster Affiliations Rune Master Demlock's Ravine Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 13 Armor 111 Experience 520 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 92-128 lvl 55 Armor 400 Experience 47,320 Elite Damage (as spell) + 154-214 lvl 83 Armor 586 Experience 1,266,200 The Rune Casters are a witch-like cult who have taken up residence in Demlock's Ravine where, led by the Rune Master, they summon Rune Beasts and Rune Fiends . To fight, they either cast a stream of homing magic missiles or summon a rune monster to fight for them.

name: Rune Fiend
description: Rune Fiend Affiliations Rune Caster Demlock's Ravine Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 13 Armor 107 Experience 520 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 92-141 lvl 55 Armor 386 Experience 47,320 Elite Damage (as spell) + 154-235 lvl 83 Armor 567 Experience 1,266,200 A Rune Fiend is a creature that resembles a Minotaur-like creature with a skull of a bull with short horns. The creature is well built, covered in sandy brown fur and with hooves instead of feet. They attack by casting a red fireshot-like spell at their targets. They can be seen lurking with the Rune Casters at Demlock's Cut , along with their melee counterpart, the Rune Beast , and can also be summoned by Rune Casters like them.

name: Rune Guardian
description: Rune Guardian Affiliations Rune Master Demlock's Ravine Regular Damage (as weapon) + 75-90 lvl 15 Armor 190 Experience 696 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 375-570 lvl 57 Armor 675 Experience 63,336 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 585-878 lvl 85 Armor 990 Experience 1,694,760 The Rune Guardian is the largest of the rune monsters associated with the Rune Casters found in Demlock's Ravine, and is the one summoned by the Rune Master himself. Depending on playthrough difficulty, he wields different power tiers of the lightning-attuned Scythe of Angk .

name: Rune Master
description: Rune Master Affiliations Rune Guardian Demlock's Ravine Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 14 Armor 132 Experience 696 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 126-169 lvl 56 Armor 470 Experience 63,336 Elite Damage (as spell) + 210-282 lvl 84 Armor 689 Experience 1,694,760 The Rune Master is the leader of the witch-like Rune Casters and their beasts. He and his Rune Guardian block access to the long rope bridge that spans Demlock's Ravine and prevent the Hero of Arhok from reaching Lhuk Torath .

name: Rusted Automaton
description: Rusted automatons are a model of Utraean war machine found in the ruins of Mhakesha . They are armed with a 3-barreled machine gun fed by a curved box magazine, providing ranged fire support protect their blade-equipped Weathered Automaton counterparts alongside Ancient Scorpiots .

name: Sand Basher
description: Sand Basher Affiliations Imp Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe Canyons Regular Damage 170 - 190 Armor 362 Experience 34,000 Veteran Damage 289 - 395 Armor 714 Experience 170,000 Elite Damage 401 - 548 Armor 991 Experience 4,964,000 The Sand Basher is a class of Imp in Dungeon Siege . These desert-dwelling demons operate alongside Rippers and Sand Mages to slay travelers. The 'Bashers' are stronger than the 'Rippers,' and both have a tendency to guard the squishier but more dangerous Mages. Much like other Imps Sand Bashers are diminutive humanoids; grey-skinned with short pants to match, they have large, elaborate horns and opalescent blue eyes.

name: Sand Mage
description: Sand Mage Affiliations Imp Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe Canyons Regular Damage 130 - 150 Armor 344 Experience 30,000 Veteran Damage 226 - 319 Armor 709 Experience 150,000 Elite Damage 314 - 443 Armor 984 Experience 4,380,000 The Sand Mage is a class of Imp in Dungeon Siege . This desert demon usually operates in packs and is supported by hand-to-hand fighter imps, Sand Rippers and Sand Bashers . Their spell, sand mage blast , projects a silent, powerful burst of heat that tracks enemies through the sand. It can deal heavy damage-over-time and cannot be evaded. Sand Mages are beige-skinned, have large horns, and wear green underpants. They often appear from nowhere in a swirling cloud of flame.

name: Sand Ripper
description: Sand Ripper Affiliations Imps Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe Canyons Regular Damage 120 - 140 Armor 300 Experience 22,000 Veteran Damage 209 - 298 Armor 611 Experience 154,000 Elite Damage 290 - 413 Armor 848 Experience 4,268,000 The Sand Ripper is a class of Imp in Dungeon Siege . These desert-dwelling demons operate alongside Sand Bashers and Sand Mages to slay travelers. The 'Rippers' are not as strong as the 'Bashers' and lack their elaborate horns; both have a tendency to guard the squishier but more dangerous Mages. Sand Rippers are diminutive humanoids in appearance: muscular, brown-skinned with yellow extremities, have large orange compound eyes, and green underpants.

name: Sandskreech
description: Sandskreech A sandskreech Affiliations Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 66-70 lvl 14 Armor 101 Experience 460 Veteran Damage h2h 184-238 lvl 56 Armor 351 Experience 55,660 Elite Damage h2h 267-346 lvl 84 Armor 510 Experience 1,491,780 The Sandskreech is a one-off arthropod found in Xot's Pass . It resembles a dark greenish Picker with purple growths.

name: Scaly Rahk
description: Scaly Rahk Affiliations Cicatrix Lair of Cicatrix Undead Drakes Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 12 h2h 80-90 lvl 12 Armor 142 Experience 388 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 216-297 lvl 54 h2h 288-396 lvl 54 Armor 604 Experience 46,948 Elite Damage (as spell) + 360-495 lvl 82 h2h 432-594 lvl 82 Armor 906 Experience 1,258,284 The Scaly Rahk is a slightly stronger version of the normal Rahk that has a purple belly.

name: Scorpion
description: This giant-sized scorpion hunts in the dwarven mining sites in the single- and multiplayer maps. It has the immediately-recognizeable pincers, tail, and other tagmata of scorpions, but a brilliant red-orange carapace and big, stupid grin that might be considered less typical of the species.

name: Scorpiot Gunner
description: Scorpiot Gunner Have a picture of Scorpiot Gunner? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Goblins Great Clock Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 19 Armor 162 Experience 1,793 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 116-163 lvl 62 Armor 461 Experience 69,927 Elite Damage (as spell) + 192-268 lvl 90 Armor 646 Experience 1,868,306 Scorpiot Gunners are a scorpion-resembling robot found in the Great Clock facility. They are armed with three weapons: a fireshot launcher on one pincer, a Gatling burst cannon on the other pincer, and a purple laser beam cannon on the tail-stinger.

name: Scorpiot Slasher
description: Scorpiot Slasher Affiliations Great Clock Regular Damage h2h 93-93 lvl 19 Armor 212 Experience 2,391 Veteran Damage h2h 217-265 lvl 62 Armor 591 Experience 93,249 Elite Damage h2h 303-371 lvl 90 Armor 827 Experience 2,491,422 Scorpiot Slashers are melee-based Scorpiots found in the Great Clock facility that are armed with a triple-threat set of blades; two on the front arms and one on the tail. They are complemented in ranged combat by the Scorpiot Gunner .

name: Scrub Howler
description: Scrub Howler Three Scrub Howlers in Xot's Badlands Affiliations Nosirrom Regular Damage (as weapon) + 80-94 lvl 16 Armor 145 Experience 460 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 355-537 lvl 58 Armor 499 Experience 55,660 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 549-820 lvl 86 Armor 724 Experience 1,491,780 Scrub Howlers were uplifted baboons intended by Nosirrom to be soldiers of his with which he could destroy the Utraeans . They were found in Xot's Badlands . Scrub Howlers resemble enormous mandrills. These bipedal creatures are very tall and well-built, towering over human-sized folk. Their fur is a dark ochre with white belly. Their hairless faces have elongated muzzle with red stripe down the middle and blue ridges on the sides. In addition, their buttocks are bare. Scrub Howlers truly lives up to their names, since they can be heard howling during the battle, which is possibly a war cry in order to demoralise their foes. They carries a big and long staff with a crescent moon-shaped blade at the end, which is used for hacking away their unfortunate foes into several pieces. Despite their large sizes, Scrub Howlers can hack away at incredible speed with their staffs. They sound like dying Sand Ripper when triggered.

name: Scrub Troll
description: The Scrub Troll is a large, lanky ape-like creature with brownish-yellowish fur. They have yellow eyes, small pointy ears and have unusually long forearms. They can be encountered at Xot’s Badlands. They will attack any unsuspecting adventurers with their immense pack, overwhelming them if they do not disperse them quickly enough. For you, Troll King 98.

name: Sea Mangler
description: The sea mangler is a highly-aggressive, amphibious sea creature inhabiting temperate waters around the main continent of Aranna. Sea manglers habitually make landfall and bask on the open sand in large groups. When their population booms, sea manglers are a ready, if dangerous, source of good-quality meat for coastal settlements like Meren . Fishermen had already cleared them out by the time adventurers arrived in the town on the quest to unite the Townstones, leaving the heroes with less-edible foes such as Trolls and Bandits. All manglers have the general appearance of a deep-sea fish, with a scaly body, a huge, toothy maw and gullet, and fins to allow motion underwater. They are also equipped with clawed forearms that allow them to quickly crawl overland. While other types of manglers have one eye, the sea mangler has two small eyes, perhaps to allow some limited depth-perception in the open sea.

name: Seck Archer
description: Like other Seck, the archers are grey-skinned, muscular humanoids. An archer wears finely-wrought pantaloons that are drab in color and not protective: in battle, he relies on his standard Seck Bow and high constitution. The Seck race is referenced (without being named) in many cutscenes during the Ehb storyline (the Seck Resurgence ), always as an "ancient evil": to wit, the Seck Archer is much bigger and tougher than a hero character of his level, and in groups, the Seck pose a dire threat to the party. Archers are encountered in Castle Ehb and the Vault of Eternity .

name: Seck Elite Archer
description: Seck Elite Archer Affiliations Seck Vault of Eternity Regular Damage as weapon + (140 - 190) Armor 510 Experience 69,000 Veteran Damage as weapon + (309 - 545) Armor 1,111 Experience 207,000 Elite Damage as weapon + (470 - 805) Armor 1,510 Experience 6,417,000 The Seck Elite Archer is a type of foe in Dungeon Siege . These units are encountered at the entrance to the Vault of Eternity and thereafter, as members of the Seck commander Gom's vanguard. They have the same appearance as Elite Guards . They have superior armor and damage output to Seck Archers , but strange to say, their life and experience values are lower.

name: Seck Elite Guard
description: Seck Elite Guard Affiliations Seck Vault of Eternity Regular Damage as weapon + (45 - 80) Armor 688 Experience 150,000 Veteran Damage as weapon + (153 - 319) Armor 1,285 Experience 450,000 Elite Damage as weapon + (268 - 507) Armor 1,756 Experience 13,950,000 The Seck Elite Guard is a type of foe in Dungeon Siege . These powerful melee combatants are encountered at the entrance to the Vault of Eternity and thereafter. Occasionally they will drop their Seck Elite Sword when slain. The Elite Guards sport a different uniform from the Grunts and have a considerably higher defense rating.

name: Seck Grunt
description: Like other Seck, the Grunt is a grey-skinned, muscular humanoid. His garments are finely-wrought but drab in color and not protective. In battle the grunt relies on his melee fighting skill and high constitution. The Seck race is referenced (without being named) in many cutscenes during the Ehb storyline (the Seck Resurgence ), always as an "ancient evil": to wit, even the grunt is much bigger and tougher than a hero character of his level, and in groups, the Seck pose a dire threat to the party. The Grunts are encountered in Castle Ehb and the Vault of Eternity .

name: Seck High Mage
description: This is a stronger version of the Seck Mage . Its life, damage output, and the experience awarded for attacking it are all markedly greater. The High Mage is a grey-skinned, muscular humanoid like other Seck, seen wearing elaborate clothing and a cape. They wield Seck Staves but favor the Flame Blades spell in combat. They are concentrated towards the end of the Vault of Eternity, sworn not to outlive their master Gom .

name: Seck Mage
description: Like other Seck, the mage is a grey-skinned, muscular humanoid. He wears a finely-wrought cape and body suit. The mages wield Seck Staves but favor the Flame Blades spell in combat. The Seck race is referenced (without being named) in many cutscenes during the Ehb storyline (the Seck Resurgence ), always as an "ancient evil": to wit, the Seck Mage is much bigger and tougher than a hero character of his level, and in groups, the Seck pose a dire threat to the party. Magi are encountered in Castle Ehb and the Vault of Eternity .

name: Shadow Lunger
description: Covered in strange magenta patterns, this creature is easy to tell apart from the regulars. It drops either a magic armor piece or a wildcard item (armor, weapons, or jewelry) when vanquished.

name: Shadowjumper
description: Shadowjumper Race Unknown Gender Male Affiliations Shadow Great Clock Class Mage Location Great Clock Loot Random Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 30 Armor 482 Health 2,984 Experience 4,048 Veteran Damage (as spell) lvl 76 Armor 1,343 Health 7,886 Experience 89,056 Elite Damage (as spell) lvl 104 Armor 1,852 Health 7,886 Experience 2,404,512 Sound File:Shadowjumper Voice.ogg The Shadowjumper is a shadowy sorcerer and the main antagonist of Legends of Aranna . He stole the Staff of Stars after slaughtering a caravan outside of Arhok , near the Arhok Cold Storage Cave . He planned to slay every Utraean until they were extinct.

name: Shadowjumper Lieutenant
description: Shadowjumper Lieutenants are the personal bodyguard of the Shadowjumper , protecting him during the Hero of Arhok's final showdown around the Great Clock . They are armed with a katana with a unique blue lightning enchantment, only obtainable by use of the Death Blast glitch .

name: Shard
description: Shards are found in the Subterranean River passage in Ehb and the Crystal Caverns on the Peninsula . On Aranna , the Shadowjumper sets miniboss Shards, named Crystal Guardians , to impede the hero's progress to a Displacer Pad that would get his team closer to the Great Clock. They are not much of a threat, as they deal little damage and if one circles, they can avoid their homing projectiles; and can break them on a stalagmite. They pose most threat in singleplayer, where micromanagement of this kind can be tricky. Shards idle on the ground as a pretty but inert pile of broken crystals. As the player approaches, the crystals coalesce into a body core and four pronged legs. How and why they are hostile to the player, but not to the other inhabitants of their cave networks, is never made clear. They are one of the unique, "cool" features that draw people into the caves. One would be disappointed traveling through the Sulfur Tunnels, though. Although the many present yellow crystals and subterranean river are a sight to see, there are no more than 3 shards total; all purple, near the Lang side of the cave. Basic Shards come in 4 varieties; listed from weakest to strongest:

name: Shrack
description: Shrack Affiliations Water Dungeon Flooded Sanctuary Regular Damage h2h 180-200 lvl 8 Armor 190 Experience 4,000 Veteran Damage h2h 345-468 lvl 51 Armor 446 Experience 88,000 Elite Damage h2h 486-660 lvl 84 Armor 628 Experience 2,376,000 Shrack Have a picture of Shrack? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Shrack is a species of hostile fish that dwells in the flooded underground ruins. They swim around in cages; after an adventurer opens one by walking nearby, the Shrack will wait a few seconds before rushing out and attacking.

name: Skank Witch
description: This creature wears red rags instead of the customary green ones, and radiates a deathly purple aura of power. The Skank Witch can drop a magic item of extreme value when slain. There is a 99.4% chance of either random armor coverage, a mage robe, or a wildcard item, but the range defined for the parameterized content is so high on this creature that the roll might fail. The ranges are still not quite as high as those of the other, stronger Swamp Witch miniboss, The Lost Witch .

name: Skeleton
description: Skeleton Affiliations Undead Regular Damage (as weapon) + 7-8 lvl 1 Armor 29 Experience 16 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 225-233 lvl 53 Armor 550 Experience 17,648 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 343-382 lvl 88 Armor 800 Experience 616,848 The Skeleton is an enemy archetype and a playable race in Dungeon Siege . This is the description of the Skeletons according to the Microsoft Games Website [ 1 ]

name: Skeleton Archer
description: Skeleton Archer Affiliations Undead Regular Damage (as weapon) + 9-11 lvl 0 Armor 28 Experience 33 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 161-281 lvl 0 Armor 280 Experience 21,945 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 255-444 lvl 0 Armor 434 Experience 608,157 The Skeleton Archer is a low level skeletal enemy encountered in the earlier stages of Dungeon Siege.

name: Skeleton Captain
description: Skeleton Captain Affiliations Regular Damage (as weapon) + 3-6 lvl 5 Armor 39 Experience 40 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 200-220 lvl 57 Armor 540 Experience 40,600 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 330-352 lvl 91 Armor 830 Experience 999,160 The Skeleton Captain refers to two undead enemies in Dungeon Siege that share the same name, found in Ehb and the Utraean Peninsula . The lower-level one can be found in each region's spider dungeons, and the higher-level one can be found in each region's dry grasslands. Both can be found surrounded by other skeletal minions.

name: Skeleton Guard
description: Skeleton Guard Affiliations Undead Skeletons Regular Damage (as weapon) + 8-10 lvl 3 Armor 37 Experience 20 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 203-238 lvl 55 Armor 495 Experience 31,080 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 330-390 lvl 89 Armor 792 Experience 799,640 The skeleton guard forms the (if you will excuse the pun) backbone of the skeletal army the player will face in the earlier stages of Dungeon Siege .

name: Skeleton Krug Dog
description: Skeleton Krug Dog Affiliations Skeletons Fortress Kroth / Iliarth Canyon Regular Damage h2h 130-134 lvl 21 Armor 350 Experience 13,000 Veteran Damage h2h 231-291 lvl 62 Armor 730 Experience 130,000 Elite Damage h2h 323-407 lvl 95 Armor 1,020 Experience 3,328,000 Skeleton Krug Dogs are a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege . These animated canine skeletons are not affiliated with the Krug . They are far stronger than living Krug Dogs and are encountered long after the player has moved past Krug-infested areas, in dry grasslands like Iliarth Canyon and the lands around Fortress Kroth , alongside higher-level skeleton warriors . Skeleton Krug Dogs are usually found in large packs and use that tactic to overwhelm adventurers with their jaws and teeth. Level 61 combat mages can summon one to fight on their behalf. Despite their appearance, they may still have internal organs intact; if dismemberment is turned on, they leave behind a cloud of red vapor when killed. This is likely an oversight.

name: Skeleton Mercenary
description: Skeleton Mercenary Affiliations Undead Furies Dragons Gresh Regular Damage as weapon + (90 - 120) Armor 480 Experience 17,980 Veteran Damage as weapon + (184 - 327) Armor 954 Experience 125,860 Elite Damage as weapon + (269 - 480) Armor 1533 Experience 3,488,120 The Skeleton Mercenary is an Undead enemy in Dungeon Siege . These are the melee brothers of the Skeleton Ranger ; Empire of Stars or 10th Legion swordsmen that died long ago in battle, and were revived by necromancy.

name: Skeleton Ranger
description: Skeleton Ranger Affiliations Undead Furies Dragons Gresh Regular Damage as weapon + (100 - 119) Armor 312 Experience 12,000 Veteran Damage as weapon + (251 - 387) Armor 641 Experience 120,000 Elite Damage as weapon + (388 - 585) Armor 896 Experience 3,072,000 The Skeleton Ranger is an Undead enemy in Dungeon Siege .

name: Skick
description: Skick Affiliations Subterranean River Crystal Caverns Regular Damage h2h 75-85 lvl 11 Armor 150 Experience 781 Veteran Damage h2h 248-344 lvl 62 Armor 624 Experience 53,889 Elite Damage h2h 369-511 lvl 96 Armor 927 Experience 1,430,792 Skick Affiliations Healing Well Regular Damage h2h 85-104 lvl 18 Armor 147 Experience 696 Veteran Damage h2h 219-328 lvl 61 Armor 457 Experience 63,336 Elite Damage h2h 312-467 lvl 89 Armor 651 Experience 1,694,760 Skicks are a group of spindly humanoid beings with four long spider-like arms and two legs. These creatures have small head with a large single red eye. They use their long arms to move and attack the players by slicing them with their claws rapidly.

name: Skitterclaw
description: Skitterclaw Have a picture of Skitterclaw? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Hostile wildlife Demlock's Cut Demlock's Ravine Regular Damage h2h 74-82 lvl 13 Armor 140 Experience 520 Veteran Damage h2h 222-300 lvl 55 Armor 496 Experience 47,320 Elite Damage h2h 325-441 lvl 83 Armor 728 Experience 1,266,200 The Skitterclaw is an ostrich-sized flightless bird with two long legs and bright red feathers. These flightless birds inhabit the highlands of the island. They usually appears in flocks with their juvenile form, Skitterlings . They are extremely aggressive towards any other creatures and wouldn't hesistate to use their large beaks and talons to inflict serious wounds.

name: Skitterling
description: Skitterling Affiliations Hostile wildlife Demlock's Cut Demlock's Ravine Regular Damage h2h 67-73 lvl 13 Armor 101 Experience 348 Veteran Damage h2h 201-267 lvl 55 Armor 366 Experience 42,108 Elite Damage h2h 294-392 lvl 83 Armor 537 Experience 1,128,564 The Skitterling is an enemy found on Island of the Utraeans , in the temperate environs surrounding Kmethekt's Tower . They are a purple-feathered avian that appear to be a juvenile version of the larger Skitterclaw .

name: Slag
description: Slags are lava golems summoned by Gom during his final battle in the Vault of Eternity . Level 105 combat mages can summon them for themselves, if they have the mana to do so.

name: Slarg
description: Slarg Affiliations Subterranean River Crystal Caverns Regular Damage h2h 50-54 lvl 14 Armor 69 Experience 299 Veteran Damage h2h 182-241 lvl 66 Armor 290 Experience 48,438 Elite Damage h2h 272-359 lvl 100 Armor 434 Experience 1,295,567 The Slarg is a strange amphibian that lives in Subterranean River and crystalline caves. The creature resembles a green skinned mudskipper with short "horns" and two arms on the side. Slarg will attack the player with retractable sphincter maw that fold in and out of the body.

name: Snapper
description: The snapper is a highly-aggressive, amphibious creature native to temperate zones. While significantly smaller than its sea and swamp cousins, this mangler subspecies may be a dangerous farmland pest on account of its habitat, which includes inland bodies of water used as irrigation reserves. All manglers have the general appearance of a deep-sea fish, with a scaly body and a huge maw and gullet, fins to allow motion underwater, and a single eye. Unfortunately, thinking them limited to water movement is a dangerous error, for the creatures are also equipped with clawed forearms that allow them to quickly crawl overland in pursuit of their target. Snappers are encountered in groups. While they seem to enjoy basking on open ground, they will also lie concealed underwater or within brush, watching for prey.

name: Snow Boar
description: Snow Boar Have a picture of Snow Boar? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The snow boar is a variant of Aranna's Hell Boar that is found in its northwest, near the town of Arhok . Members of the species participated in the Shadowjumper's attack on the Staff of Stars caravan.

name: Snow Wolf
description: Snow Wolf Affiliations Canids Snow Regions Regular Damage 13 - 17 Armor 22 Experience 10 Veteran Damage 503 - 804 Armor 1046 Experience 11,030 Elite Damage 830 - 1327 Armor 1727 Experience 448,030 The Snow Wolf is a type of wolf encountered in Legends of Aranna . The other wolves, in ascending order of strength, are:

name: Soul Stinger
description: Soul Stinger Affiliations Cliffs of Fire Quillrabe Canyons Mesa Desert Regular Damage 125 - 145 Armor 250 Experience 11,000 Veteran Damage 217 - 308 Armor 534 Experience 143,000 Elite Damage 302 - 428 Armor 742 Experience 3,729,000 The Soul Stinger is a hostile desert insect in Dungeon Siege . This is a flying, stinging insect that delivers somewhat more-penetrating hits than the Giant Wasps also found in their habitats. Together the Wasp and Stinger are the strongest winged insects, but relatively weak among the desert creatures.

name: Spider
description: In addition to the many species of monstrous oversized spiders found throughout Aranna, there is a smaller species that resembles the larger Yellow Spider ; this harmless species is about the size of a tarantula and can be found crawling around in the first few rooms of Wesrin Cross .

name: Spiked Dweller
description: Spiked Dweller Affiliations Hostile wildlife Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 0 Armor 300 Experience 5,450 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 165-222 lvl 0 Armor 673 Experience 119,900 Elite Damage (as spell) + 289-388 lvl 0 Armor 951 Experience 3,237,300 The Spiked Dweller is a creature found in Redwood Gap and its Ehb analogue near Bonepicker's Post and the Temple Ruins . It is the greater cousin of the Spiked Maw . Its high exp:health ratio makes it a bountiful source of leveling experience.

name: Spiked Maw
description: This squat, toadlike creature is usually encountered in caves and forests and is a lesser cousin of the Spiked Dweller . The back of the Maw is laden with steely-grey spikes, and the Dweller's spikes are bright green. The beast attacks by leaning over and presenting its back, from which it ejects a cluster of spikes via the spell spiked_maw_spikes . The combination of its high-damage attack plus poor health and armor ratings make the Spiked Maw a priority target. Its high exp:health ratio makes it a bountiful source of experience.

name: Stalagnid
description: Stalagnid Have a picture of Stalagnid? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Stalagnid are a group of spiders that lives in the caves and disguise as a stalagmite. When an unwitting adventurer approaches, Stalagnid will unfurls itself into a large spider with green marking on its abdomen, and then attacks. They usually comes in small group so it's best to keep them at their distance. They can be found in the cavern part of The Halls of the Lost .

name: Stingbot
description: Stingbot Affiliations Goblins Great Clock Regular Damage h2h 120-136 lvl 21 Armor 212 Experience 1,793 Veteran Damage h2h 280-387 lvl 64 Armor 591 Experience 69,927 Elite Damage h2h 392-543 lvl 92 Armor 827 Experience 1,868,306 Stingbots are robot versions of the Synged that can be found in the Great Clock facility.

name: Swamp Creature
description: These great lumbering monsters inhabit the muck beneath Lang Mire and the Dark Swamp . Their health and damage ratings are very high. They can be conjured and controlled by Swamp Witches .

name: Swamp Slinger
description: These are diminutive shambling creatures that inhabit Lang Mire and the Dark Swamp . They have the same mesh as the Swamp Creature but are very small in comparison, and have a green hue. Associating in small groups, the creatures hurl noxious muck at foes. They can be conjured and controlled by Swamp Witches .

name: Swamp Stinger
description: The Swamp Stinger is a fair example of the giant, stinging flies that the residents of Ehb and the Peninsula exclaim over when discussing the swamplands. The creature has the glistening blue carapace and faceted eyes of an out-sized housefly, but is predatory in nature and will attack humans relentlessly.

name: Swamp Troll
description: Swamp Troll Have a picture of Swamp Troll? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Swamp Troll are a group of miniature trolls with yellow skin and carries a club. The creature lives in the depth of the swamp, crawling along the rotting wooden bridges and near water. They comes in large group, proving to be quite dangerous despite their small sizes.

name: Swamp Witch
description: Covens of these creatures practice the dark arts within the unpleasant obscurity provided by the swamp. Appearing as a plodding, hunchbacked old woman in rags, the witch is a dangerous spellcaster. She wears a dark hooded cloak and have claw-like hands. Her spell ice_mage_balls is long-ranged and effective, and the witch can maintain and replace summoned creatures indefinitely. The summon will be a Swamp Creature or Swamp Slinger , at random. When approached, the witch will begin to retreat, causing the player to disengage automatically and need to be re-ordered. Expect more enemies to be drawn into battle when chasing the witch through the swamp. When killed, the swamp witch's skeleton will emerge out of her body and rise up in the air for a moment before vanishing.

name: Swanny the Mad Jailer
description: Swanny is the burly overseer of the King's dungeons underneath Castle Ehb . He sports a heavy mustache, a green leather jerkin, an executioner's hood, and a thick wooden bat which he drops when slain. While there are indications that Swanny had a malevolent nature (not the least of which being that he attacks the party once sprung from his torture cage), he ultimately validated his king's trust, since he did not betray the Chamber of Stars to the Seck during their interrogation.

name: Swarmling Drone
description: Swarmling Drone Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 13 Armor 131 Experience 348 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 231-226 lvl 55 Armor 466 Experience 42,108 Elite Damage (as spell) + 385-376 lvl 83 Armor 684 Experience 1,128,564 Swarmling drones are an insectoid enemy found in the Island of the Utraeans , specifically in Demlock's Ravine . They are red-carapaced, bite enemies with their mouthparts, and protect a Swarmling Queen .

name: Swarmling Queen
description: Swarmling Queens are a green-carapaced arthropod enemy found on the Island of the Utraeans , specifically in Demlock's Ravine , in the general area of the Tower of Kmethekt . They possess a ranged breath attack, and are protected by biting Swarmling Drones .

name: Swirling Midges
description: Swirling Midges Have a picture of Swirling Midges? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Affiliations Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 13 Armor 131 Experience 348 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 231-226 lvl 55 Armor 466 Experience 42,108 Elite Damage (as spell) + 385-376 lvl 83 Armor 684 Experience 1,128,564 Swirling Midges are an enemy found on the Island of the Utraeans . They fire a ranged homing spell attack at their targets.

name: Synged
description: Synged Affiliations Vault of Eternity Volcanic Caverns Regular Damage 230 - 250 Armor 300 Experience 35,000 Veteran Damage 361 - 480 Armor 576 Experience 175,000 Elite Damage 495 - 658 Armor 789 Experience 5,110,000 The Synged is a hostile creature in Dungeon Siege . These are demonic, fleshy flying creatures with large pedipalps and a huge compound eye. They are typically found in volcanic areas in Western Aranna.

name: Syrrus
description: The Syrrus is a saurian monster that inhabits subterranean lakes. It remains mostly submerged when attacking, so its size and shape are open to speculation, but what does emerge is not very reassuring. The creature has a long and very strong neck many times the size of a human, scaled in brilliant blue-green hues. Its head is draconic, with an elongated mouth, scaled plumage, and horns. The Syrrus attacks exclusively with its breath, which appears to be a cone of super-chilled caustic mist. It deals high damage-over-time in a large area in front of the attack trajectory.

name: Teal Shard
description: This is a Shard -type creature of slightly lesser size and strength encountered in the crystal cave environments— a weaker specimen than the Green , Blue , and Purple Shards . Its spell, shard_zap_teal , is a homing-projectile attack similar to that used by other shards.

name: Terror Wraith
description: Terror Wraiths are high-grade melee fighters that may be under the sway of Seck or Maljin forces when encountered. They wear a crusty old helmet and carry a Blunt Assault Axe (82-112) with which they can express their opinion about the player. Wraiths in general are cursed spirits that have been animated by a nearby evil presence or the necromantic arts. The lower half of their body is gone and the creature is essentially a skull, arms, ribcage, and spine. It floats in the air and moves with much greater speed and purpose than a full-bodied Skeleton .

name: Tesla Coil
description: Tesla Coil A four-armed tesla coil of the type that guards the Goblin Inventor. The Tesla Coils are a group of machines that remain still until they sense the adventurers nearby and release a powerful electrical blast. To do this, they rotate themselves fast enough to powering up an electricity and emits a deadly bolt of lightning. Tesla Coil can be found standing stationary inside the Ehb lair of the Goblin Inventor , where they usually guard entrances to rooms. Most machines resemble an oversized Lightning Gun with rings and two arms in the shape of the cross, though three 4-armed version protect the Goblin Inventor and the chamber where he kept Merik's staff. The two-armed ones discharge a blue-colored lightning bolt, while the four-armed ones discharge red.

name: The Lost Witch
description: This is one of the two Swamp Witch minibosses. The Lost Witch wears distinctly-ragged green shawls and has charcoal skin, glowing eyes, and a deathly-purple aura of power. As with other swamp witches, she employs a spell that randomly summons a Swamp Creature or Swamp Slinger for ten minutes, in addition to her own ranged attack spell, ice_mage_balls (whose damage is modified by the listed values). The Lost Witch of Ehb is a hermit who tends a secret garden manse on the border between the Dark Forest and Dark Swamp. On the peninsula, the Lost Witch of Lang Mire is a member of an arcane coven due north-northeast of town.

name: Toreck
description: Toreck Affiliations Castle Ehb Castle Hiroth Regular Damage as weapon + (40 - 60) Armor 440 Experience 50,000 Veteran Damage as weapon + (137 - 253) Armor 851 Experience 200,000 Elite Damage as weapon + (236 - 403) Armor 1,166 Experience 4,650,000 The Toreck is a hostile monster in Dungeon Siege . This brutish humanoid creature resembles a minotaur with tusks and wearing a loin. It stands upright on ungulate legs and wields a high-strength polearm, a Sickle Staff. Its movement speed is moderate and its attack rate is relentless. When there are demonic invasions in the Dungeon Siege storylines, these creatures are very often on the scene.

name: Tortured Corpse
description: Tortured Corpse Affiliations Undead Wesrin Cross Hovart's Folly Regular Damage h2h 10-13 lvl 2 Armor 8 Experience 54 Veteran Damage h2h 70-133 lvl 56 Armor 90 Experience 25,650 Elite Damage h2h 111-194 lvl 90 Armor 141 Experience 699,030 The tortured corpse is an undead enemy encountered early in Dungeon Siege .

name: Tretch
description: Tretch Affiliations Lang Mire Eastern Swamp Regular Damage h2h 140-144 lvl 18 Armor 260 Experience 1,450 Veteran Damage h2h 350-440 lvl 66 Armor 726 Experience 75,400 Elite Damage h2h 504-633 lvl 99 Armor 1,046 Experience 2,002,450 Tretch are a group of tall sentient trees that lives in the steamy swamp. They resembles a dead rotting tree with massive yellow eye and have two flexible branches that can be used as arms. When there are no preys hanging around, Tretch usually sways as if it was pushed by the winds while waiting for an unsuspecting adventurers walking past. When attacking, Tretch will swipe their branches and lashes at their foes. When killed, Tretch will drops its branches to its sides and drooped as if it just withers.

name: Trog
