description: Trog Affiliations Subterranean River Crystal Caverns Sulfur Tunnels Regular Damage (as weapon) + 18-22 lvl 12 Armor 78 Experience 392 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 156-312 lvl 64 Armor 342 Experience 47,432 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 250-497 lvl 98 Armor 514 Experience 1,271,256 The Trog are a race of beings who inhabit the enchanted crystal-lined cave systems of Aranna. They are monstrous-looking hunchbacked people with disproportionately thin limbs compared to their torsos. They have solid yellow eyes and what appears to be a beaked mouth with teeth. Their numbers were once kept in check by the Legions stationed in their lands, but the crises plaguing said lands in the games' storylines forced the Legions to withdraw, leading to an upsurge in Trog numbers and activity. They could possibly be sapient, smart enough to form hierarchies and use weapons and armor as a normal person would, even magic ones. However, a lack of conversable Trog NPCs limits the ability to prove this. They usually let out a horrifying moaning and groaning upon being killed.

name: Trog Knight
description: Trog Knight Have a picture of Trog Knight? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Trog Knights are the most heavily armed and armored Trog to be found in Dungeon Siege . They wear scale armor and heft a Trog Great Sword.

name: Trog Warrior
description: Trog Warrior Have a picture of Trog Warrior? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Trog Warriors are the medium line infantry of the Trog , dressed in some kind of brigandine and armed with a sword and a Metal Buckler.

name: Troll Masher
description: Troll Masher Have a picture of Troll Masher? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Troll Masher is a massive giant with green skin and wear a spotted loincloth, which is probably made of deer fur or even a Hassat skin. Troll Masher is durable, and can inflict high damage with its fists. It can be found among smaller trolls in the lands of the Great Clock , on the slopes of Mount Kreth . At skill level 50, combat mages can transform themselves into a Troll Masher, granted they have the mana for it.

name: Unguis
description: Unguis Affiliations Water dungeons Flooded Sanctuary Regular Damage h2h 140-150 lvl 34 Armor 550 Experience 12,000 Veteran Damage h2h 268-351 lvl 77 Armor 1,243 Experience 120,000 Elite Damage h2h 378-495 lvl 110 Armor 1,750 Experience 3,072,000 The Unguis is a hostile monster that can be found in waterlogged structures. It sends up a cephalopod tentacle that attacks passers-by. If one damages this tentacle enough, the Unguis dies under the floor.

name: Ursae
description: Ursae Have a picture of Ursae? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Ursae is an undead beast found in Legends of Aranna . It is a rotting and decomposing bear that can be found in the swampy foothills on both sides of the Mountain of the Dead , in the Island of the Utraeans Fen of the Frozen region. Combat mages of level 25 or higher may summon an Ursae to fight for them.

name: Varg
description: This creature inhabits snowy regions on mainland Aranna, and in particular is seen on the outskirts of the town of Arhok . Vargs are small wolf-relatives, and appear to be solitary hunters of small game. When excited, they emit higher-pitched growls and yips that would embarrass a full-sized wolf. The aura of the Shadowjumper drove the vargs and various other regional fauna into a frenzy. Vargs began attacking humans, whom they would normally avoid due to a vast difference in size. These are very nearly the first foes encountered when the hero steps out of the door. Again, they are less pack-oriented than normal wolves, and do not pose much of a threat even in this early stage.

name: Venom Fiend
description: This is a miniboss Scorpion . The Venom Fiend's unique markings glow red-orange in the dark. There are two instances of this creature: the one in Ehb is down an elevator shaft in the Glitterdelve Mine , near an accumulation of water, and the one on the Peninsula is in a neglected stretch of the Miner's Haven in the Crystwind Mines .

name: Vile Mucosa
description: Vile Mucosa Affiliations Mucosae Regular Damage as spell + (2 - 4) Armor 27 Experience 39 Veteran Damage as spell + (18 - 44) Armor 305 Experience 25,935 Elite Damage as spell + (28 - 69) Armor 469 Experience 718,731 Vile Mucosa Have a picture of Vile Mucosa? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies The Vile Mucosa is a variant of the Mucosa found in the earlier stages of play in Dungeon Siege .

name: Walking Corpse
description: Walking Corpse Affiliations Wesrin Cross Hovart's Folly Undead Regular Damage h2h 9-13 lvl 3 Armor 41 Experience 22 Veteran Damage h2h 61-123 lvl 57 Armor 423 Experience 17,930 Elite Damage h2h 105-161 lvl 90 Armor 656 Experience 604,604 A Walking Corpse is an undead enemy faced in Dungeon Siege .

name: Warrior Wraith
description: The Warrior Wraith is stronger than its compatriot Battle Wraiths. It radiates a purple aura of power and delivers savage melee attacks. There are two of these creatures; one in Ehb and the other on the Utraean Peninsula , and both of them were evidently victims of the Furies , as they are in the monsters' general proximity. In these ways the Warrior Wraith is similar to the Wraith Mage miniboss, the Dark Wraith Mage .

name: Water Elemental
description: These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . A great Water Elemental and two minions were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. The water elemental is a hulking, horned biped with the general shape of a Larch . The resemblance ends at the shape, as rather than being a creature of flesh, the elemental is a congelated animus of water. The elemental is berserk and will attack with its giant fists. Anyone who has done a belly-flop may be able to appreciate how hard water can hit.

name: Water Elemental Minion
description: These spirits were bound to a material existence by the elemental magic of the Great Clock . Two Water Elemental Minions were compelled by the Shadowjumper to attack the Hero of Arhok , simply to buy time. The Minions are relatively small, taking the form of common Manglers found in watery places throughout Aranna. Nevertheless, their glistening aqua sheen and unearthly strength marked them as beings far removed from the common mangler.

name: Water Lunger
description: Water Lungers and Water Slingers colonize flooded interior zones. Both of these creatures are extremely dangerous. The Lunger is a dark grey skinned hand-to-hand fighter.

name: Water Slinger
description: Water Slingers and Water Lungers colonize flooded interior zones. Their attacks are extremely strong, and as a result these areas are considered very dangerous. The Slinger is a blue-green creature that uses the rapid-fire spell water_lunger_spear , launching what is either a small javelin, or a very large fork, at the victim.

name: Weathered Automaton
description: Weathered automatons are a model of Utraean war machine found in the ruins of Mhakesha . They are armed with a long blade on each arm, providing a front line to protect their machinegun-equipped Rusted Automaton counterparts, as well as Ancient Scorpiots .

name: White Wolf
description: White Wolf Affiliations Canids Snow Regions Regular Damage 33 - 37 Armor 55 Experience 87 Veteran Damage 175 - 240 Armor 420 Experience 30,363 Elite Damage 267 - 366 Armor 639 Experience 938,730 The White Wolf is a type of wolf encountered in Dungeon Siege . The other wolves, in ascending order of strength, are:

name: Wraith
description: Wraiths are disembodied skeletons that have been reanimated by the malign influence of some greater being. They lack a lower half to their body and are essentially a skull, arms, ribcage, and spine. Be that as it may, wraiths float in the air and move much faster than Skeletons , and like all undead, are single-minded, fearless attackers. Wraiths are mid- to very-high-level foes. They are first seen in Fury dens and may be encountered in dark interior regions thereafter. The lesser Wraith is a melee fighter, and can be recognized by its studded steel cap and two-handed Field Axe. Wraiths are usually encountered as piles of bones strewn across the ground. If something draws near, the bones quickly assemble in midair and charge it down. Level 50 combat mages can summon one to fight on their behalf.

name: Wraith Archer
description: Wraiths are cursed spirits that have been animated by a nearby evil presence or the necromantic arts. The lower half of their body is gone and the creature is essentially a skull, arms, ribcage, and spine. It floats in the air and moves with much greater speed and purpose than a full-bodied Skeleton . Wraith archers use a Long Backed Bow (94-110, 13m range) in combat. Like other undead beings, they are single-minded, fearless attackers. Level 57 combat mages can summon one to fight on their behalf.

name: Wraith Mage
description: Wraith Magi cast a strange, enervating spell called "wraith hands" that homes in on the target and deals heavy damage to its life force. The projectile moves slowly but intelligently, and makes for a frustrating thing to dodge. Fortunately, their usual habitats often have significant cover to harmlessly break the projectile on. Wraiths (in general) are cursed spirits that have been animated by a nearby evil presence or the necromantic arts. The lower half of their body is gone and the creature is essentially a skull, arms, ribcage, and spine. It floats in the air and moves with much greater speed and purpose than a full-bodied Skeleton .

name: Wraith Piercer
description: Wraith Piercers are high-grade ranged combatants that may be under the sway of Seck or Maljin forces when encountered. They draw a Seck Bow (121-149) in service to whatever evil called them forth. Wraiths in general are cursed spirits that have been animated by a nearby evil presence or the necromantic arts. The lower half of their body is gone and the creature is essentially a skull, arms, ribcage, and spine. It floats in the air and moves with much greater speed and purpose than a full-bodied Skeleton .

name: Wraith Warlock
description: Warlocks are high-level spellcasters that may be under the sway of Seck or Maljin forces when encountered. Their thunderous "wraith zap" spell has a very long range, deals serious damage, and cannot be evaded. Be prepared to take a beating unless you can target the warlock right away and close in. Wraiths (in general) are cursed spirits that have been animated by a nearby evil presence or the necromantic arts. The lower half of their body is gone and the creature is essentially a skull, arms, ribcage, and spine. It floats in the air and moves with much greater speed and purpose than a full-bodied Skeleton .

name: Yellow Eye
description: Yellow Eye Have a picture of Yellow Eye? Then please upload it! For more information please consult our image policies Yellow Eye is a big bear that can be found in the Snowy Canyon to the north of Arhok . It is the first boss-type monster that can be encountered by the Hero of Arhok, and it guards a treasure chest in the canyon below the bridge where Jondar can be found. His presence prevented the trapper Kron from being able to access the traps by which Arhok could get fresh meat, so he sent the Hero on a mission to kill the beast. When they returned with news of their victory, Kron rewarded them with 90 gold.

name: Yellow Spider
description: Yellow Spider A yellow Spider Affiliations Wesrin Cross Hovart's Folly Regular Damage h2h 1-1 lvl 3 Armor 5 Experience 5 Veteran Damage h2h 9-11 lvl 57 Armor 70 Experience 6,740 Elite Damage h2h 14-17 lvl 90 Armor 108 Experience 330,815 The Yellow Spider is an small spider that can be encountered in Wesrin Cross and Hovart's Folly . The arachnid is a pus yellow colour and about as big as a small crate, they are spawned from larger webs which are destructible and can sometimes be seen walking along the tops of walls and then descend down their webs to attack the player.

name: Yeti
description: The yeti is a shaggy, towering humanoid that is based on the ones in folklore just like its stronger cousin the Furok , covered in thick white fur and with huge hands with claws to rip its prey apart with sheer force. It is found in Arhok Cold Storage Cave .

name: Zaurask Adept
description: Zaurask Adept Affiliations Zaurask Jherkal's Crown Central Transportation Center Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 12 Armor 102 Experience 392 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 137-196 lvl 54 Armor 402 Experience 47,432 Elite Damage (as spell) + 228-327 lvl 82 Armor 596 Experience 1,271,256 Zaurask Adepts are the second tier of Zaurask combat magic-users, found in and around Jherkal's Crown. Unlike the others, the Adepts carry a staff, probably a Zaurask Trident, or maybe a puller staff/cruel staff.

name: Zaurask Archer
description: Zaurask Archer Affiliations Zaurask Beaches Regular Damage (as weapon) + 22-30 lvl 5 Armor 50 Experience 120 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 410-617 lvl 47 Armor 329 Experience 35,280 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 675-1002 lvl 75 Armor 513 Experience 945,240 Zaurask Archers are the lowest and earliest-found tier of bow-using Zaurask . They are found in the beachy environs surrounding Illicor , as well as within the city itself.

name: Zaurask Claw
description: Zaurask Claw Affiliations Zaurask Beaches Regular Damage h2h 50-60 lvl 5 Armor 64 Experience 120 Veteran Damage h2h 281-412 lvl 47 Armor 416 Experience 35,280 Elite Damage h2h 438-643 lvl 75 Armor 650 Experience 945,240 Zaurask Claws are a class of Zaurask basic infantry; the weakest ones and the earliest found. They are completely unarmed and unarmored, relying on their scaly hides, natural weapons, and strong physiques to carry them through battles. They are found in the beachy environs in and around Illicor .

name: Zaurask Fang
description: Zaurask Fang Affiliations Zaurask Jherkal's Crown Central Transportation Center Regular Damage h2h 74-82 lvl 13 Armor 138 Experience 403 Veteran Damage h2h 241-327 lvl 55 Armor 532 Experience 48,763 Elite Damage h2h 358-484 lvl 83 Armor 790 Experience 1,306,929 Zaurask Fangs are the second tier of Zaurask basic infantry encountered. Like the preceding Claw , it uses beastfu to fight. Unlike it, however, the Claw wears a piece of armor, namely a left shoulder plate. They are found occupying the stone understructures of the ruined city of Jherkal's Crown as well as the Central Transportation Center below.

name: Zaurask Fighter
description: Zaurask Fighter Affiliations Fortress Emarard Zaurask Regular Damage (as weapon) + 95-104 lvl 20 Armor 212 Experience 1,473 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 412-576 lvl 63 Armor 626 Experience 76,596 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 633-868 lvl 91 Armor 888 Experience 2,034,213 Zaurask Fighters are the third and highest tier of Zaurask elite melee infantry to be encountered on the Island of the Utraeans , forming part of Nosirrom's vanguard in Fortress Emarard and using turtle-shell shields. They and the Slasher are essentially texture-swapped from the previous tier of infantry.

name: Zaurask Incanter
description: Zaurask Incanter Affiliations Zaurask Beaches Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 5 Armor 50 Experience 120 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 236-347 lvl 47 Armor 329 Experience 35,280 Elite Damage (as spell) + 394-578 lvl 75 Armor 513 Experience 945,240 Zaurask Incanters are the lowest-leveled and earliest-found class of Zaurask spellcasters, using the regular red Zaurask fireshot at level 5 power. They are found in the beachy areas in and around the city of Illicor .

name: Zaurask Leader
description: Zaurask Leader Affiliations Xulphae's Cove Healing Well Zaurask Regular Damage (as weapon) + 102-119 lvl 18 Armor 222 Experience 1,281 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 478-713 lvl 61 Armor 692 Experience 66,612 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 729-1069 lvl 88 Armor 980 Experience 1,769,061 Zaurask Leaders are the second class of Zaurask elite infantry encountered, a local superior to the Zaurask Whacker . They are the first Zaurask class to be seen wearing the characteristic turtle-shell shield. If killed on Veteran difficulty while using Death Blast, they have a chance of dropping their Tempered Blade, a sword with too much DP10S for its low strength requirement.

name: Zaurask Lightning
description: Zaurask Lightning Affiliations Regular Damage (as weapon) + 44-53 lvl 16 Armor 91 Experience 655 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 246-367 lvl 59 Armor 297 Experience 59,605 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 386-563 lvl 87 Armor 423 Experience 1,594,925 Zaurask Lightnings are the third class of Zaurask archers to be encountered. They are the ranged physical component of the Zaurask forces that have encircled Xulphae's Cove and tainted the Healing Well .

name: Zaurask Mage
description: Zaurask Mage Affiliations Fortress Emarard Zaurask Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 20 Armor 184 Experience 1,656 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 166-221 lvl 63 Armor 548 Experience 86,112 Elite Damage (as spell) + 274-364 lvl 91 Armor 777 Experience 2,286,936 Zaurask Mages are the highest tier of normal Zaurask ranged spellcaster. They are found among Nosirrom's closest army in and around Fortress Emarard .

name: Zaurask Ranger
description: Zaurask Ranger Affiliations Fortress Emarard Zaurask Regular Damage (as weapon) + 81-90 lvl 20 Armor 150 Experience 1,244 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 341-483 lvl 63 Armor 453 Experience 64,688 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 521-727 lvl 91 Armor 643 Experience 1,717,964 Zaurask Rangers are the highest tier of Zaurask archers encountered in Legends of Aranna , providing physical ranged support for Nosirrom's forces in Fortress Emarard .

name: Zaurask Seer
description: Zaurask Seer Affiliations Xulphae's Cove Healing Well Zaurask Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 17 Armor 118 Experience 977 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 128-195 lvl 60 Armor 374 Experience 67,413 Elite Damage (as spell) + 211-323 lvl 88 Armor 533 Experience 1,789,864 Zaurask Seers are the third tier of Zaurask mages to be encountered, providing ranged magic support for their melee and bow brethren around Xulphae's Cove.

name: Zaurask Slasher
description: Zaurask Slasher Affiliations Fortress Emarard Zaurask Regular Damage (as weapon) + 79-88 lvl 20 Armor 238 Experience 1,213 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 348-514 lvl 63 Armor 699 Experience 63,076 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 535-779 lvl 91 Armor 991 Experience 1,675,153 Zaurask Slashers are the highest-encountered tier of Zaurask basic infantryman, forming the melee bulk of Nosirrom's forces in Fortress Emarard . They have the appearance of the Leaders before them, but do not carry a shield.

name: Zaurask Slavemaster
description: Zaurask Slavemaster Affiliations Zaurask Jherkal's Crown Central Transportation Center Regular Damage (as weapon) + lvl 11 Armor 150 Experience 514 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + lvl 53 Armor 576 Experience 46,774 Elite Damage (as weapon) + lvl 81 Armor 854 Experience 1,251,590 Zaurask Slavemasters are the first encountered class of Zaurask elite infantry, found in and around Jherkal's Crown. They wear significantly more coverings than other Zaurask, and are the first encountered that fight with melee weapons.

name: Zaurask Spine
description: Zaurask Spine Affiliations Zaurask Jherkal's Crown Central Transportation Center Regular Damage (as weapon) + 45-57 lvl 11 Armor 89 Experience 398 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 248-397 lvl 53 Armor 355 Experience 48,158 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 390-617 lvl 81 Armor 526 Experience 1,290,714 Zaurask Spines are the second tier of Zaurask bow users encountered in the Legends of Aranna campaign. They provide physical ranged support for the Zaurask forces occupying Jherkal's Crown.

name: Zaurask Whacker
description: Zaurask Whacker Affiliations Xulphae's Cove Zaurask Regular Damage (as weapon) + 95-104 lvl 18 Armor 210 Experience 982 Veteran Damage (as weapon) + 364-509 lvl 61 Armor 638 Experience 67,758 Elite Damage (as weapon) + 550-761 lvl 89 Armor 909 Experience 1,799,024 Zaurask Whackers are the third level of basic Zaurask infantry encountered. They are the first Zaurask infantryman to carry a melee weapon, a sword.

name: Zepheryl
description: Zepheryl Affiliations Tower of Kmethekt Regular Damage (as spell) lvl 13 Armor 50 Experience 348 Veteran Damage (as spell) + 120-210 lvl 55 Armor 176 Experience 42,108 Elite Damage (as spell) + 199-351 lvl 83 Armor 259 Experience 1,128,564 Zepheryls are a class of entity composed of red magical energy that attack enemies by zapping them. Level 23 nature mages can summon them for themselves, given enough mana. They have a larger cousin in the form of the Dark Zepheryl . They are perhaps related to Demlock's Orb spells.

name: Ztrool
description: Ztrool Affiliations Ramparts Hostile wildlife Regular Damage h2h 68-77 lvl 19 Armor 142 Experience 798 Veteran Damage h2h 170-236 lvl 62 Armor 431 Experience 55,062 Elite Damage h2h 242-335 lvl 90 Armor 611 Experience 1,461,936 Ztrools are a creature found on the natural ramparts of Fortress Emarard . They appear to be smaller versions of the Chompers found in the Island of the Utraeans Southern Coast , except unlike them, Ztrools hide out in rocks like Scandinavian trolls. Level 40 combat mages can transform into Ztrools via spell.
